12: 
13: # JavaScript Code Quality Checklist
14: 
15: Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Specific quality checks for JavaScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.
24: 
25: ### Priority Levels
26: 
27: | Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
28: |-------|---------|-------------|
29: | P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
30: | P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
31: | P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS
36: 
37: These items MUST be fixed before any commit.
38: 
39: ### Box Header
40: 
41: ```markdown
42: [ ] File has box header with component identification
43: ```
44: 
45: **Required format**:
46: ```javascript
47: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
48: // ║ COMPONENT: [Component Name]                                               ║
49: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
50: // ║ PURPOSE: [Brief description of what this file does]                       ║
51: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
52: ```
53: 
54: ### 'use strict'
55: 
56: ```markdown
57: [ ] 'use strict' directive present at top of file
58: ```
59: 
60: **Required** for `.js/.cjs` files (immediately after header):
61: ```javascript
62: 'use strict';
63: ```
64: 
65: **Exception**: `.mjs` files and OpenCode plugin ESM paths are strict by module
66: semantics; the verifier intentionally skips `.mjs` strict-mode enforcement.
67: 
68: ### Function Naming
69: 
70: ```markdown
71: [ ] All functions use camelCase naming
72: ```
73: 
74: **Correct**:
75: ```javascript
76: function validateInput(data) { }
77: function processFile(path) { }
78: const handleError = (err) => { };
79: ```
80: 
81: **Wrong**:
82: ```javascript
83: function validate_input(data) { }    // snake_case
84: function ProcessFile(path) { }       // PascalCase
85: ```
86: 
87: ### No Commented-Out Code
88: 
89: ```markdown
90: [ ] No commented-out code blocks
91: ```
92: 
93: **Remove**:
94: ```javascript
95: // function old_implementation() {
96: //     return "deprecated";
97: // }
98: ```
99: 
100: ### WHY Comments
101: 
102: ```markdown
103: [ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
104:     - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
105:     - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
106: ```
107: 
108: **Required for**:
109: - Algorithm choices
110: - Performance optimizations
111: - Business rule implementations
112: - Workarounds for known issues
113: 
114: ### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections
115: 
116: ```markdown
117: [ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
118: ```
119: 
120: ---
121: 
122: ## 3. P1 - REQUIRED
123: 
124: These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.
125: 
126: ### Module Exports by Path
127: 
128: ```markdown
129: [ ] Module export style matches the file path and package boundary
130: ```
131: 
132: **Correct for non-plugin `.js/.cjs` utilities**:
133: ```javascript
134: module.exports = { functionName, CONSTANT };
135: module.exports = functionName;
136: ```
137: 
138: **Correct for OpenCode plugin loader paths**:
139: ```javascript
140: export default async function ExamplePlugin(ctx, options = {}) {
141:   return {};
142: }
143: ```
144: 
145: **Plugin paths**: Files in `.opencode/plugins/` and
146: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` MUST use ESM
147: (`export default`) — see `quality_standards.md` §10 OpenCode Plugin Exemption
148: Tier.
149: 
150: **Wrong for non-plugin CommonJS utilities**:
151: ```javascript
152: export { function_name };
153: export default function_name;
154: ```
155: 
156: ### Guard Clauses
157: 
158: ```markdown
159: [ ] Functions use early returns for invalid states
160: ```
161: 
162: **Correct**:
163: ```javascript
164: function processData(data) {
165:     if (!data) return null;
166:     if (!data.requiredField) return null;
167: 
168:     // Main logic here
169:     return result;
170: }
171: ```
172: 
173: **Wrong**:
174: ```javascript
175: function processData(data) {
176:     if (data) {
177:         if (data.requiredField) {
178:             // Deeply nested logic
179:         }
180:     }
181: }
182: ```
183: 
184: ### Bracketed Logging
185: 
186: ```markdown
187: [ ] Console output uses bracketed format
188: ```
189: 
190: **Correct**:
191: ```javascript
192: console.log('[COMPONENT] Processing file:', filename);
193: console.error('[COMPONENT] Error:', error.message);
194: ```
195: 
196: **Wrong**:
197: ```javascript
198: console.log('Processing file: ' + filename);
199: console.log(filename);  // No context
200: ```
201: 
202: ### Constants
203: 
204: ```markdown
205: [ ] Module-level constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
206: ```
207: 
208: **Correct**:
209: ```javascript
210: const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
211: const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5000;
212: const VALID_TYPES = ['a', 'b', 'c'];
213: ```
214: 
215: ### Error Handling
216: 
217: ```markdown
218: [ ] Try/catch blocks handle errors appropriately
219: [ ] Error messages include context
220: ```
221: 
222: **Correct**:
223: ```javascript
224: try {
225:     const data = JSON.parse(content);
226: } catch (error) {
227:     console.error('[PARSER] Failed to parse JSON:', error.message);
228:     throw new Error(`Invalid JSON in ${filename}: ${error.message}`);
229: }
230: ```
231: 
232: ### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks
233: 
234: ```markdown
235: [ ] KISS: avoid unnecessary abstraction or layering
236: [ ] DRY: repeated rules/constants extracted to shared source
237: [ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations checked and documented
238: ```
239: 
240: ---
241: 
242: ## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED
243: 
244: These improve quality but can be deferred.
245: 
246: ### JSDoc Comments
247: 
248: ```markdown
249: [ ] Public functions have JSDoc documentation
250: ```
251: 
252: **Example**:
253: ```javascript
254: /**
255:  * Validates input data against schema.
256:  * @param {Object} data - The data to validate
257:  * @param {Object} schema - Validation schema
258:  * @returns {Object} Validation result with isValid and errors
259:  */
260: function validateInput(data, schema) { }
261: ```
262: 
263: ### Consistent String Quotes
264: 
265: ```markdown
266: [ ] Strings use single quotes consistently
267: ```
268: 
269: **Preferred**:
270: ```javascript
271: const message = 'Hello world';
272: const config = { key: 'value' };
273: ```
274: 
275: ### Async/Await Pattern
276: 
277: ```markdown
278: [ ] Async operations use async/await (not callbacks where possible)
279: ```
280: 
281: **Preferred**:
282: ```javascript
283: async function fetchData() {
284:     const response = await fetch(url);
285:     const data = await response.json();
286:     return data;
287: }
288: ```
289: 
290: ### Destructuring
291: 
292: ```markdown
293: [ ] Object destructuring used where it improves clarity
294: ```
295: 
296: **Good**:
297: ```javascript
298: const { name, value } = config;
299: function process({ data, options = {} }) { }
300: ```
301: 
302: ---
303: 
304: ## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE
305: 
306: For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as JavaScript surface evidence.
307: 
308: ```markdown
309: ## JavaScript Standards Surface Evidence
310: 
311: - [ ] JavaScript standards validated in `javascript_checklist.md`
312: - [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
313: - [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
314: - [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
315: - [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
316: ```
317: 
318: ---
319: 
320: ## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS
321: 
322: ```bash
323: # Syntax check
324: node --check file.js
325: 
326: # ESLint (if configured)
327: npx eslint file.js
328: 
329: # Check for common issues in non-plugin CommonJS utilities
330: grep -n "function [a-z]*_[a-z]" file.js  # Find snake_case functions
331: grep -n "^export " file.js              # Review: allowed only for ESM/plugin paths
332: ```
333: 
334: ---
335: 
336: ## 7. RELATED RESOURCES
337: 
338: ### Checklists
339: - [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) - Language-agnostic checks
340: 
341: ### Style Guide
342: - [JavaScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/javascript/style_guide.md)
343: - [JavaScript Quality Standards](../../../references/opencode/javascript/quality_standards.md)

(End of file - total 343 lines)
</content>

---
title: JavaScript Code Quality Checklist
description: Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
trigger_phrases:
  - "opencode javascript checklist"

---

# JavaScript Code Quality Checklist

Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Specific quality checks for JavaScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.

### Priority Levels

| Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
| P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
| P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |

---

## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS

These items MUST be fixed before any commit.

### Box Header

```markdown
[ ] File has box header with component identification
```

**Required format**:
```javascript
// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
// ║ COMPONENT: [Component Name]                                               ║
// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
// ║ PURPOSE: [Brief description of what this file does]                       ║
// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### 'use strict'

```markdown
[ ] 'use strict' directive present at top of file
```

**Required** for `.js/.cjs` files (immediately after header):
```javascript
'use strict';
```

**Exception**: `.mjs` files and OpenCode plugin ESM paths are strict by module
semantics; the verifier intentionally skips `.mjs` strict-mode enforcement.

### Function Naming

```markdown
[ ] All functions use camelCase naming
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
function validateInput(data) { }
function processFile(path) { }
const handleError = (err) => { };
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
function validate_input(data) { }    // snake_case
function ProcessFile(path) { }       // PascalCase
```

### No Commented-Out Code

```markdown
[ ] No commented-out code blocks
```

**Remove**:
```javascript
// function old_implementation() {
//     return "deprecated";
// }
```

### WHY Comments

```markdown
[ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
    - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
    - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
```

**Required for**:
- Algorithm choices
- Performance optimizations
- Business rule implementations
- Workarounds for known issues

### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections

```markdown
[ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
```

---

## 3. P1 - REQUIRED

These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.

### Module Exports by Path

```markdown
[ ] Module export style matches the file path and package boundary
```

**Correct for non-plugin `.js/.cjs` utilities**:
```javascript
module.exports = { functionName, CONSTANT };
module.exports = functionName;
```

**Correct for OpenCode plugin loader paths**:
```javascript
export default async function ExamplePlugin(ctx, options = {}) {
  return {};
}
```

**Plugin paths**: Files in `.opencode/plugins/` and
`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` MUST use ESM
(`export default`) — see `quality_standards.md` §10 OpenCode Plugin Exemption
Tier.

**Wrong for non-plugin CommonJS utilities**:
```javascript
export { function_name };
export default function_name;
```

### Guard Clauses

```markdown
[ ] Functions use early returns for invalid states
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
function processData(data) {
    if (!data) return null;
    if (!data.requiredField) return null;

    // Main logic here
    return result;
}
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
function processData(data) {
    if (data) {
        if (data.requiredField) {
            // Deeply nested logic
        }
    }
}
```

### Bracketed Logging

```markdown
[ ] Console output uses bracketed format
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
console.log('[COMPONENT] Processing file:', filename);
console.error('[COMPONENT] Error:', error.message);
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
console.log('Processing file: ' + filename);
console.log(filename);  // No context
```

### Constants

```markdown
[ ] Module-level constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5000;
const VALID_TYPES = ['a', 'b', 'c'];
```

### Error Handling

```markdown
[ ] Try/catch blocks handle errors appropriately
[ ] Error messages include context
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
try {
    const data = JSON.parse(content);
} catch (error) {
    console.error('[PARSER] Failed to parse JSON:', error.message);
    throw new Error(`Invalid JSON in ${filename}: ${error.message}`);
}
```

### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks

```markdown
[ ] KISS: avoid unnecessary abstraction or layering
[ ] DRY: repeated rules/constants extracted to shared source
[ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations checked and documented
```

---

## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED

These improve quality but can be deferred.

### JSDoc Comments

```markdown
[ ] Public functions have JSDoc documentation
```

**Example**:
```javascript
/**
 * Validates input data against schema.
 * @param {Object} data - The data to validate
 * @param {Object} schema - Validation schema
 * @returns {Object} Validation result with isValid and errors
 */
function validateInput(data, schema) { }
```

### Consistent String Quotes

```markdown
[ ] Strings use single quotes consistently
```

**Preferred**:
```javascript
const message = 'Hello world';
const config = { key: 'value' };
```

### Async/Await Pattern

```markdown
[ ] Async operations use async/await (not callbacks where possible)
```

**Preferred**:
```javascript
async function fetchData() {
    const response = await fetch(url);
    const data = await response.json();
    return data;
}
```

### Destructuring

```markdown
[ ] Object destructuring used where it improves clarity
```

**Good**:
```javascript
const { name, value } = config;
function process({ data, options = {} }) { }
```

---

## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE

For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as JavaScript surface evidence.

```markdown
## JavaScript Standards Surface Evidence

- [ ] JavaScript standards validated in `javascript_checklist.md`
- [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
- [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
- [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
- [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
```

---

## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS

```bash
# Syntax check
node --check file.js

# ESLint (if configured)
npx eslint file.js

# Check for common issues in non-plugin CommonJS utilities
grep -n "function [a-z]*_[a-z]" file.js  # Find snake_case functions
grep -n "^export " file.js              # Review: allowed only for ESM/plugin paths
```

---

## 7. RELATED RESOURCES

### Checklists
- [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) - Language-agnostic checks

### Style Guide
- [JavaScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/javascript/style_guide.md)
- [JavaScript Quality Standards](../../../references/opencode/javascript/quality_standards.md)

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: ---
2: title: TypeScript Code Quality Checklist
3: description: Quality validation checklist for TypeScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "opencode typescript checklist"
6:   - "typescript quality validation checklist"
7:   - "no any public api"
8:   - "pascalcase types check"
9: importance_tier: normal
10: contextType: implementation
11: ---
12: 
13: # TypeScript Code Quality Checklist
14: 
15: Quality validation checklist for TypeScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Specific quality checks for TypeScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.
24: 
25: ### Priority Levels
26: 
27: | Level | Meaning       | Enforcement                        |
28: |-------|---------------|------------------------------------|
29: | P0    | HARD BLOCKER  | Must fix before commit             |
30: | P1    | Required      | Must fix or get explicit approval  |
31: | P2    | Recommended   | Can defer with justification       |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS
36: 
37: These items MUST be fixed before any commit.
38: 
39: ### File Header
40: 
41: ```markdown
42: [ ] File has TypeScript module header identifying the module
43: ```
44: 
45: **Required format**:
46: ```typescript
47: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48: // MODULE: [Module Name]
49: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50: ```
51: 
52: ### No `any` in Public API
53: 
54: ```markdown
55: [ ] No `any` type used in exported functions, interfaces, or type aliases
56: ```
57: 
58: **Correct**:
59: ```typescript
60: export function parse(input: unknown): SearchConfig { }
61: export interface SearchOptions { metadata: Record<string, unknown>; }
62: ```
63: 
64: **Wrong**:
65: ```typescript
66: export function parse(input: any): any { }
67: export interface SearchOptions { metadata: any; }
68: ```
69: 
70: **Exception**: Interop with untyped third-party modules requires justification comment:
71: ```typescript
72: // Interop: sqlite-vec has no type definitions
73: // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
74: const sqliteVec = require('sqlite-vec') as any;
75: ```
76: 
77: ### PascalCase Types and Interfaces
78: 
79: ```markdown
80: [ ] All interfaces, type aliases, and enums use PascalCase naming
81: ```
82: 
83: **Correct**:
84: ```typescript
85: interface SearchResult { }
86: type MemoryState = 'active' | 'archived';
87: enum ErrorCode { NotFound, Timeout }
88: ```
89: 
90: **Wrong**:
91: ```typescript
92: interface searchResult { }       // camelCase
93: type memoryState = string;       // camelCase
94: enum errorCode { }               // camelCase
95: ```
96: 
97: ### No Commented-Out Code
98: 
99: ```markdown
100: [ ] No commented-out code blocks present
101: ```
102: 
103: Same rule as JavaScript. Delete unused code; git preserves history.
104: 
105: ### WHY Comments
106: 
107: ```markdown
108: [ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
109:     - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
110:     - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
111: ```
112: 
113: ### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections
114: 
115: ```markdown
116: [ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
117: ```
118: 
119: Same rule as JavaScript. Explain reasoning, not mechanics.
120: 
121: ---
122: 
123: ## 3. P1 - REQUIRED
124: 
125: These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.
126: 
127: ### Explicit Return Types on Public Functions
128: 
129: ```markdown
130: [ ] All exported functions have explicit return type annotations
131: ```
132: 
133: **Correct**:
134: ```typescript
135: export function calculate(input: number): number { }
136: export async function search(query: string): Promise<SearchResult[]> { }
137: ```
138: 
139: **Wrong**:
140: ```typescript
141: export function calculate(input: number) { }      // inferred return
142: export async function search(query: string) { }    // inferred return
143: ```
144: 
145: Private/internal helper functions may use inferred return types.
146: 
147: ### Interfaces for Data Shapes
148: 
149: ```markdown
150: [ ] Object shapes passed across module boundaries use named interfaces
151: ```
152: 
153: **Correct**:
154: ```typescript
155: interface SearchOptions {
156:   limit: number;
157:   offset: number;
158: }
159: function search(query: string, options: SearchOptions): void { }
160: ```
161: 
162: **Wrong**:
163: ```typescript
164: function search(query: string, options: { limit: number; offset: number }): void { }
165: ```
166: 
167: ### Strict Mode Enabled
168: 
169: ```markdown
170: [ ] tsconfig.json has "strict": true
171: ```
172: 
173: No partial strict mode. All strict flags must be enabled.
174: 
175: ### Module System Matches Package Boundary
176: 
177: ```markdown
178: [ ] TypeScript module settings match the owning workspace/package
179: ```
180: 
181: **Current system-spec-kit baseline**:
182: - `shared/` and `mcp_server/`: package `"type": "module"` plus
183:   `module: "nodenext"` and `moduleResolution: "nodenext"`.
184: - `scripts/`: package `"type": "module"` plus `module: "es2022"` and
185:   `moduleResolution: "node"`.
186: - Root `tsconfig.json`: CommonJS fallback for workspaces that do not override it.
187: 
188: Do not describe CommonJS output as the universal TypeScript baseline.
189: 
190: ### No Non-Null Assertions Without Justification
191: 
192: ```markdown
193: [ ] Non-null assertions (!) have a justification comment on the preceding line
194: ```
195: 
196: **Correct**:
197: ```typescript
198: // Map guaranteed to contain key after initialization phase (line 42)
199: const value = map.get(key)!;
200: ```
201: 
202: **Wrong**:
203: ```typescript
204: const value = map.get(key)!;  // No explanation
205: ```
206: 
207: ### TSDoc on Public API
208: 
209: ```markdown
210: [ ] Exported functions, classes, and interfaces have TSDoc comments
211: ```
212: 
213: **Minimum**:
214: ```typescript
215: /** Search memories matching the given query. */
216: export async function searchMemories(query: string): Promise<SearchResult[]> { }
217: ```
218: 
219: **Full (for complex functions)**:
220: ```typescript
221: /**
222:  * Search memories matching the given query.
223:  *
224:  * @param query - Search query text
225:  * @param options - Search configuration
226:  * @returns Matching memories sorted by relevance
227:  * @throws {@link MemoryError} If database unavailable
228:  */
229: ```
230: 
231: ### Typed Error Handling
232: 
233: ```markdown
234: [ ] catch blocks use `unknown` type and narrow with instanceof
235: ```
236: 
237: **Correct**:
238: ```typescript
239: try { } catch (error: unknown) {
240:   if (error instanceof MemoryError) { }
241: }
242: ```
243: 
244: **Wrong**:
245: ```typescript
246: try { } catch (error) {        // implicitly any
247:   console.log(error.message);  // unsafe access
248: }
249: ```
250: 
251: ### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks
252: 
253: ```markdown
254: [ ] KISS: remove unnecessary abstraction layers
255: [ ] DRY: duplicate constants/rules are centralized
256: [ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP risks reviewed with evidence
257: ```
258: 
259: ---
260: 
261: ## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED
262: 
263: These improve quality but can be deferred.
264: 
265: ### Utility Types Used Where Appropriate
266: 
267: ```markdown
268: [ ] Standard utility types preferred over manual type construction
269: ```
270: 
271: **Correct**:
272: ```typescript
273: function update(overrides: Partial<Config>): Config { }
274: type Summary = Pick<MemoryRecord, 'id' | 'text'>;
275: ```
276: 
277: **Less optimal**:
278: ```typescript
279: function update(overrides: { timeout?: number; limit?: number }): Config { }
280: // Manual partial instead of Partial<Config>
281: ```
282: 
283: ### Discriminated Unions for Complex State
284: 
285: ```markdown
286: [ ] Multi-state objects use discriminated unions instead of optional properties
287: ```
288: 
289: **Correct**:
290: ```typescript
291: type Result<T> =
292:   | { success: true; data: T }
293:   | { success: false; error: string };
294: ```
295: 
296: **Less optimal**:
297: ```typescript
298: interface Result<T> {
299:   success: boolean;
300:   data?: T;         // unclear when present
301:   error?: string;   // unclear when present
302: }
303: ```
304: 
305: ### Type-Only Imports Separated
306: 
307: ```markdown
308: [ ] Imports used only in type positions use `import type`
309: ```
310: 
311: **Correct**:
312: ```typescript
313: import { MemoryError } from './errors';          // runtime value
314: import type { SearchResult } from '../types';     // type only
315: ```
316: 
317: **Less optimal**:
318: ```typescript
319: import { MemoryError, SearchResult } from './module';
320: // SearchResult only used as type but imported as value
321: ```
322: 
323: ### Generic Constraints Where Applicable
324: 
325: ```markdown
326: [ ] Generic type parameters have constraints when meaningful
327: ```
328: 
329: **Correct**:
330: ```typescript
331: function findById<T extends { id: string }>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined { }
332: ```
333: 
334: **Less optimal**:
335: ```typescript
336: function findById<T>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined { }
337: // No guarantee T has an 'id' property
338: ```
339: 
340: ### Readonly Where Appropriate
341: 
342: ```markdown
343: [ ] Configuration objects and immutable data use readonly modifiers
344: ```
345: 
346: **Correct**:
347: ```typescript
348: interface SearchConfig {
349:   readonly maxResults: number;
350:   readonly timeout: number;
351: }
352: ```
353: 
354: ---
355: 
356: ## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE
357: 
358: For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as TypeScript surface evidence.
359: 
360: ```markdown
361: ## TypeScript Standards Surface Evidence
362: 
363: - [ ] TypeScript standards validated in `typescript_checklist.md`
364: - [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
365: - [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
366: - [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
367: - [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
368: ```
369: 
370: ---
371: 
372: ## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS
373: 
374: ```bash
375: # Type check without emitting files
376: tsc --noEmit
377: 
378: # Build with project references
379: tsc --build
380: 
381: # Check for `any` in public API
382: grep -rn ': any' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v 'node_modules'
383: 
384: # Check for missing return types on exports
385: grep -rn 'export function' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v '): '
386: 
387: # Check for untyped catch blocks
388: grep -rn 'catch (error)' src/ --include='*.ts'
389: ```
390: 
391: ---
392: 
393: ## 7. RELATED RESOURCES

---
title: TypeScript Code Quality Checklist
description: Quality validation checklist for TypeScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
trigger_phrases:
  - "opencode typescript checklist"
  - "typescript quality validation checklist"
  - "no any public api"
  - "pascalcase types check"
importance_tier: normal
contextType: implementation
---

# TypeScript Code Quality Checklist

Quality validation checklist for TypeScript code in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Specific quality checks for TypeScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.

### Priority Levels

| Level | Meaning       | Enforcement                        |
|-------|---------------|------------------------------------|
| P0    | HARD BLOCKER  | Must fix before commit             |
| P1    | Required      | Must fix or get explicit approval  |
| P2    | Recommended   | Can defer with justification       |

---

## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS

These items MUST be fixed before any commit.

### File Header

```markdown
[ ] File has TypeScript module header identifying the module
```

**Required format**:
```typescript
// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// MODULE: [Module Name]
// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

### No `any` in Public API

```markdown
[ ] No `any` type used in exported functions, interfaces, or type aliases
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
export function parse(input: unknown): SearchConfig { }
export interface SearchOptions { metadata: Record<string, unknown>; }
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
export function parse(input: any): any { }
export interface SearchOptions { metadata: any; }
```

**Exception**: Interop with untyped third-party modules requires justification comment:
```typescript
// Interop: sqlite-vec has no type definitions
// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
const sqliteVec = require('sqlite-vec') as any;
```

### PascalCase Types and Interfaces

```markdown
[ ] All interfaces, type aliases, and enums use PascalCase naming
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
interface SearchResult { }
type MemoryState = 'active' | 'archived';
enum ErrorCode { NotFound, Timeout }
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
interface searchResult { }       // camelCase
type memoryState = string;       // camelCase
enum errorCode { }               // camelCase
```

### No Commented-Out Code

```markdown
[ ] No commented-out code blocks present
```

Same rule as JavaScript. Delete unused code; git preserves history.

### WHY Comments

```markdown
[ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
    - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
    - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
```

### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections

```markdown
[ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
```

Same rule as JavaScript. Explain reasoning, not mechanics.

---

## 3. P1 - REQUIRED

These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.

### Explicit Return Types on Public Functions

```markdown
[ ] All exported functions have explicit return type annotations
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
export function calculate(input: number): number { }
export async function search(query: string): Promise<SearchResult[]> { }
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
export function calculate(input: number) { }      // inferred return
export async function search(query: string) { }    // inferred return
```

Private/internal helper functions may use inferred return types.

### Interfaces for Data Shapes

```markdown
[ ] Object shapes passed across module boundaries use named interfaces
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
interface SearchOptions {
  limit: number;
  offset: number;
}
function search(query: string, options: SearchOptions): void { }
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
function search(query: string, options: { limit: number; offset: number }): void { }
```

### Strict Mode Enabled

```markdown
[ ] tsconfig.json has "strict": true
```

No partial strict mode. All strict flags must be enabled.

### Module System Matches Package Boundary

```markdown
[ ] TypeScript module settings match the owning workspace/package
```

**Current system-spec-kit baseline**:
- `shared/` and `mcp_server/`: package `"type": "module"` plus
  `module: "nodenext"` and `moduleResolution: "nodenext"`.
- `scripts/`: package `"type": "module"` plus `module: "es2022"` and
  `moduleResolution: "node"`.
- Root `tsconfig.json`: CommonJS fallback for workspaces that do not override it.

Do not describe CommonJS output as the universal TypeScript baseline.

### No Non-Null Assertions Without Justification

```markdown
[ ] Non-null assertions (!) have a justification comment on the preceding line
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
// Map guaranteed to contain key after initialization phase (line 42)
const value = map.get(key)!;
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
const value = map.get(key)!;  // No explanation
```

### TSDoc on Public API

```markdown
[ ] Exported functions, classes, and interfaces have TSDoc comments
```

**Minimum**:
```typescript
/** Search memories matching the given query. */
export async function searchMemories(query: string): Promise<SearchResult[]> { }
```

**Full (for complex functions)**:
```typescript
/**
 * Search memories matching the given query.
 *
 * @param query - Search query text
 * @param options - Search configuration
 * @returns Matching memories sorted by relevance
 * @throws {@link MemoryError} If database unavailable
 */
```

### Typed Error Handling

```markdown
[ ] catch blocks use `unknown` type and narrow with instanceof
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
try { } catch (error: unknown) {
  if (error instanceof MemoryError) { }
}
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
try { } catch (error) {        // implicitly any
  console.log(error.message);  // unsafe access
}
```

### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks

```markdown
[ ] KISS: remove unnecessary abstraction layers
[ ] DRY: duplicate constants/rules are centralized
[ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP risks reviewed with evidence
```

---

## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED

These improve quality but can be deferred.

### Utility Types Used Where Appropriate

```markdown
[ ] Standard utility types preferred over manual type construction
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
function update(overrides: Partial<Config>): Config { }
type Summary = Pick<MemoryRecord, 'id' | 'text'>;
```

**Less optimal**:
```typescript
function update(overrides: { timeout?: number; limit?: number }): Config { }
// Manual partial instead of Partial<Config>
```

### Discriminated Unions for Complex State

```markdown
[ ] Multi-state objects use discriminated unions instead of optional properties
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
type Result<T> =
  | { success: true; data: T }
  | { success: false; error: string };
```

**Less optimal**:
```typescript
interface Result<T> {
  success: boolean;
  data?: T;         // unclear when present
  error?: string;   // unclear when present
}
```

### Type-Only Imports Separated

```markdown
[ ] Imports used only in type positions use `import type`
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
import { MemoryError } from './errors';          // runtime value
import type { SearchResult } from '../types';     // type only
```

**Less optimal**:
```typescript
import { MemoryError, SearchResult } from './module';
// SearchResult only used as type but imported as value
```

### Generic Constraints Where Applicable

```markdown
[ ] Generic type parameters have constraints when meaningful
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
function findById<T extends { id: string }>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined { }
```

**Less optimal**:
```typescript
function findById<T>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined { }
// No guarantee T has an 'id' property
```

### Readonly Where Appropriate

```markdown
[ ] Configuration objects and immutable data use readonly modifiers
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
interface SearchConfig {
  readonly maxResults: number;
  readonly timeout: number;
}
```

---

## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE

For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as TypeScript surface evidence.

```markdown
## TypeScript Standards Surface Evidence

- [ ] TypeScript standards validated in `typescript_checklist.md`
- [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
- [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
- [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
- [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
```

---

## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS

```bash
# Type check without emitting files
tsc --noEmit

# Build with project references
tsc --build

# Check for `any` in public API
grep -rn ': any' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v 'node_modules'

# Check for missing return types on exports
grep -rn 'export function' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v '): '

# Check for untyped catch blocks
grep -rn 'catch (error)' src/ --include='*.ts'
```

---

## 7. RELATED RESOURCES

### Checklists
- [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) - Language-agnostic checks

### Style Guide
- [TypeScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/typescript/style_guide.md)
- [TypeScript Quality Standards](../../../references/opencode/typescript/quality_standards.md)

2: title: Universal Code Quality Checklist

4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "universal code quality checklist"

11: ---
12: 
13: # Universal Code Quality Checklist
14: 
15: Language-agnostic quality checks that apply to all code files in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Provides baseline quality requirements that apply across all languages (JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Shell, Config). Use this checklist alongside language-specific checklists.
24: 
25: ### Priority Levels
26: 
27: | Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
28: |-------|---------|-------------|
29: | P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
30: | P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
31: | P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS
36: 
37: These items MUST be fixed before any commit.
38: 
39: ### File Header
40: 
41: ```markdown
42: [ ] File has appropriate header comment identifying the component
43:     - JavaScript: Box header with 'use strict'
44:     - TypeScript: Module header block `// ─── MODULE: NAME ───` (no 'use strict'; tsconfig handles it)
45:     - Python: Shebang + COMPONENT comment block
46:     - Shell: Shebang + COMPONENT comment block
47:     - Config: JSONC comment header
48: ```
49: 
50: **Evidence**: All style guides require headers
51: 
52: **Verifier boundary**: `verify_alignment_drift.py` checks marker-level headers
53: for supported code/config files. Exact visual style remains a manual checklist
54: gate.
55: 
56: ### No Commented-Out Code
57: 
58: ```markdown
59: [ ] No commented-out code blocks present
60:     - Use version control for code history
61:     - Explanatory comments are OK
62:     - TODO comments with context are OK
63: ```
64: 
65: **Verifier boundary**: Commented-out code and WHY-vs-WHAT comment quality are
66: manual review gates; the alignment verifier does not parse intent from comments.
67: 
68: **What to remove**:
69: ```javascript
70: // REMOVE - commented code
71: // function oldImplementation() {
72: //     return "deprecated";
73: // }
74: 
75: // KEEP - explanatory comment
76: // Note: Using setTimeout because requestAnimationFrame not available in Node
77: ```
78: 
79: ### No Ephemeral-Artifact Pointers in Comments
80: 
81: ```markdown
82: [ ] No spec/packet/phase numbers, T###/REQ-###/CHK-###, feature-catalog, ADR, or ticket ids in comments
83:     - Keep the durable WHY; drop the perishable label
84:     - Structural path/globs the code needs (e.g. .opencode/specs/) and stable standards (CWE-###) are OK
85:     - See references/universal/code_style_guide.md §4 "No ephemeral-artifact pointers"
86: ```
87: 
88: ### Numbered ALL-CAPS Section Invariant
89: 
90: ```markdown
91: [ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved
92:     - Use `## N. SECTION NAME` in standards/examples
93:     - Do not switch to sentence-case section names
94: ```
95: 
96: ### Filesystem Mutation Safety
97: 
98: ```markdown
99: [ ] File and folder mutations are guarded by canonical path checks
100:     - Use realpath/canonical path resolution for candidate and allowed roots
101:     - Use relative/boundary-safe containment checks (not substring-only checks)
102:     - Reject traversal/symlink escape attempts before create/move/delete
103: ```
104: 
105: ### Spec Folder Invariants (when touching specs)
106: 
107: ```markdown
108: [ ] Spec folder operations enforce `NNN-name` and approved roots
109:     - Allowed roots: `specs/` and `.opencode/specs/` only
110:     - Archive/restore/create targets validated before execution
111:     - Do not emit restore instructions unless checkpoint creation succeeded
112: ```
113: 
114: ---
115: 
116: ## 3. P1 - REQUIRED
117: 
118: These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.
119: 
120: ### Consistent Naming
121: 
122: ```markdown
123: [ ] Naming follows language conventions
124:     - JavaScript: camelCase functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
125:     - TypeScript: camelCase functions, PascalCase interfaces/types/enums, UPPER_SNAKE constants
126:     - Python: snake_case functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
127:     - Shell: snake_case functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
128:     - Config: camelCase keys
129: ```
130: 
131: ### WHY Comments (Manual Review)
132: 
133: ```markdown
134: [ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (P1, manual checklist gate)
135:     - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
136:     - Maximum 3 comments per 10 LOC
137:     - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
138: ```
139: 
140: **Bad (WHAT)**:
141: ```python
142: # Set x to 5
143: x = 5
144: ```
145: 
146: **Good (WHY)**:
147: ```python
148: # use 5 retries per SLA requirement for transient network failures
149: max_retries = 5
150: ```
151: 
152: ### TODO Format
153: 
154: ```markdown
155: [ ] All TODOs include context (owner or ticket number)
156: ```
157: 
158: **Bad**:
159: ```javascript
160: // TODO: fix this later
161: ```
162: 
163: **Good**:
164: ```javascript
165: // TODO(username): Add input validation
166: // TODO(TICKET-123): Handle edge case for empty arrays
167: ```
168: 
169: ### Error Messages
170: 
171: ```markdown
172: [ ] Error messages include context
173:     - What failed
174:     - Why it failed (if known)
175:     - How to fix (if applicable)
176: ```
177: 
178: **Bad**:
179: ```python
180: return False, "Invalid input"
181: ```
182: 
183: **Good**:
184: ```python
185: return False, f"Name '{name}' must be hyphen-case (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only)"
186: ```
187: 
188: ### No Magic Numbers/Strings
189: 
190: ```markdown
191: [ ] Magic values are extracted to named constants
192: ```
193: 
194: **Bad**:
195: ```javascript
196: if (items.length > 100) {  // What is 100?
197: ```
198: 
199: **Good**:
200: ```javascript
201: const MAX_ITEMS = 100;  // API rate limit per request
202: if (items.length > MAX_ITEMS) {
203: ```
204: 
205: ### KISS / DRY / SOLID Gate
206: 
207: ```markdown
208: [ ] KISS/DRY/SOLID checks applied before merge
209:     - KISS: no speculative abstraction layers
210:     - DRY: repeated rules/constants consolidated
211:     - SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations reviewed and resolved or documented
212: ```
213: 
214: ---
215: 
216: ## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED
217: 
218: These improve quality but can be deferred.
219: 
220: ### Comment Density
221: 
222: ```markdown
223: [ ] Appropriate level of documentation
224:     - Public APIs have documentation
225:     - Complex algorithms explained
226:     - Not over-commented (target <= 3 comments per 10 LOC)
227: ```
228: 
229: ### Code Organization
230: 
231: ```markdown
232: [ ] Code is organized into logical sections
233:     - Related functionality grouped
234:     - Clear separation of concerns
235:     - Consistent ordering (constants, functions, exports)
236: ```
237: 
238: ### Line Length
239: 
240: ```markdown
241: [ ] Reasonable line lengths maintained
242:     - Target: 80-100 characters
243:     - Maximum: 120 characters
244:     - Long lines broken at logical points
245: ```
246: 
247: ---
248: 
249: ## 5. VALIDATION WORKFLOW
250: 
251: ### Before Committing
252: 
253: ```markdown
254: 1. Run language-specific linter/type checker
255:    - JavaScript: ESLint or built-in checks
256:    - TypeScript: tsc --noEmit (type check without emitting)
257:    - Python: Black/flake8/mypy
258:    - Shell: ShellCheck
259: 
260: 2. Run comment-hygiene checker on each modified file
261:    - .opencode/skills/sk-code/scripts/check-comment-hygiene.sh <file>
262:    - Zero violations required
263:    - Escape: add `// hygiene-ok` to suppress a specific line
264: 
265: 3. Check this universal checklist
266:    - All P0 items must pass
267:    - P1 items addressed or approved
268: 
269: 4. Run language-specific checklist
270:    - javascript_checklist.md
271:    - typescript_checklist.md
272:    - python_checklist.md
273:    - shell_checklist.md
274:    - config_checklist.md
275: 
276: 5. Self-review
277:    - Would a colleague understand this?
278:    - Is anything overly clever?
279:    - Are edge cases handled?
280: ```
281: 
282: ### Quick Validation Commands
283: 
284: ```bash
285: # JavaScript (ESLint if available)
286: npx eslint src/
287: 
288: # TypeScript (type check without emitting)
289: tsc --noEmit
290: 
291: # Python
292: python -m flake8 scripts/
293: python -m mypy scripts/
294: 
295: # Shell
296: shellcheck *.sh
297: 
298: # Config (JSON validation)
299: python -m json.tool config.json
300: ```
301: 
302: ---
303: 
304: ## 6. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE
305: 
306: For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as cross-language surface evidence.
307: 
308: ```markdown
309: ## Cross-Language Standards Surface Evidence
310: 
311: - [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
312: - [ ] Language checklist validated for changed files
313: - [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
314: - [ ] Baseline risk checks sourced from `sk-code-review` references
315: - [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
316: ```
317: 
318: ---
319: 
320: ## 7. RELATED RESOURCES
321: 
322: ### Language-Specific Checklists
323: - [javascript_checklist.md](./javascript_checklist.md)
324: - [typescript_checklist.md](./typescript_checklist.md)
325: - [python_checklist.md](./python_checklist.md)
326: - [shell_checklist.md](./shell_checklist.md)
327: - [config_checklist.md](./config_checklist.md)
328: 
329: ### Style Guides
330: - [JavaScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/javascript/style_guide.md)
331: - [TypeScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/typescript/style_guide.md)
332: - [Python Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/python/style_guide.md)
333: - [Shell Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/shell/style_guide.md)
334: - [Config Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/config/style_guide.md)

(End of file - total 334 lines)
</content>

# Universal Code Quality Checklist

Language-agnostic quality checks that apply to all code files in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Provides baseline quality requirements that apply across all languages (JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Shell, Config). Use this checklist alongside language-specific checklists.

### Priority Levels

| Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
| P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
| P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |

---

## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS

These items MUST be fixed before any commit.

### File Header

```markdown
[ ] File has appropriate header comment identifying the component
    - JavaScript: Box header with 'use strict'
    - TypeScript: Module header block `// ─── MODULE: NAME ───` (no 'use strict'; tsconfig handles it)
    - Python: Shebang + COMPONENT comment block
    - Shell: Shebang + COMPONENT comment block
    - Config: JSONC comment header
```

**Evidence**: All style guides require headers

**Verifier boundary**: `verify_alignment_drift.py` checks marker-level headers
for supported code/config files. Exact visual style remains a manual checklist
gate.

### No Commented-Out Code

```markdown
[ ] No commented-out code blocks present
    - Use version control for code history
    - Explanatory comments are OK
    - TODO comments with context are OK
```

**Verifier boundary**: Commented-out code and WHY-vs-WHAT comment quality are
manual review gates; the alignment verifier does not parse intent from comments.

**What to remove**:
```javascript
// REMOVE - commented code
// function oldImplementation() {
//     return "deprecated";
// }

// KEEP - explanatory comment
// Note: Using setTimeout because requestAnimationFrame not available in Node
```

### No Ephemeral-Artifact Pointers in Comments

```markdown
[ ] No spec/packet/phase numbers, T###/REQ-###/CHK-###, feature-catalog, ADR, or ticket ids in comments
    - Keep the durable WHY; drop the perishable label
    - Structural path/globs the code needs (e.g. .opencode/specs/) and stable standards (CWE-###) are OK
    - See references/universal/code_style_guide.md §4 "No ephemeral-artifact pointers"
```

### Numbered ALL-CAPS Section Invariant

```markdown
[ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved
    - Use `## N. SECTION NAME` in standards/examples
    - Do not switch to sentence-case section names
```

### Filesystem Mutation Safety

```markdown
[ ] File and folder mutations are guarded by canonical path checks
    - Use realpath/canonical path resolution for candidate and allowed roots
    - Use relative/boundary-safe containment checks (not substring-only checks)
    - Reject traversal/symlink escape attempts before create/move/delete
```

### Spec Folder Invariants (when touching specs)

```markdown
[ ] Spec folder operations enforce `NNN-name` and approved roots
    - Allowed roots: `specs/` and `.opencode/specs/` only
    - Archive/restore/create targets validated before execution
    - Do not emit restore instructions unless checkpoint creation succeeded
```

---

## 3. P1 - REQUIRED

These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.

### Consistent Naming

```markdown
[ ] Naming follows language conventions
    - JavaScript: camelCase functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
    - TypeScript: camelCase functions, PascalCase interfaces/types/enums, UPPER_SNAKE constants
    - Python: snake_case functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
    - Shell: snake_case functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
    - Config: camelCase keys
```

### WHY Comments (Manual Review)

```markdown
[ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (P1, manual checklist gate)
    - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
    - Maximum 3 comments per 10 LOC
    - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
```

**Bad (WHAT)**:
```python
# Set x to 5
x = 5
```

**Good (WHY)**:
```python
# use 5 retries per SLA requirement for transient network failures
max_retries = 5
```

### TODO Format

```markdown
[ ] All TODOs include context (owner or ticket number)
```

**Bad**:
```javascript
// TODO: fix this later
```

**Good**:
```javascript
// TODO(username): Add input validation
// TODO(TICKET-123): Handle edge case for empty arrays
```

### Error Messages

```markdown
[ ] Error messages include context
    - What failed
    - Why it failed (if known)
    - How to fix (if applicable)
```

**Bad**:
```python
return False, "Invalid input"
```

**Good**:
```python
return False, f"Name '{name}' must be hyphen-case (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only)"
```

### No Magic Numbers/Strings

```markdown
[ ] Magic values are extracted to named constants
```

**Bad**:
```javascript
if (items.length > 100) {  // What is 100?
```

**Good**:
```javascript
const MAX_ITEMS = 100;  // API rate limit per request
if (items.length > MAX_ITEMS) {
```

### KISS / DRY / SOLID Gate

```markdown
[ ] KISS/DRY/SOLID checks applied before merge
    - KISS: no speculative abstraction layers
    - DRY: repeated rules/constants consolidated
    - SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations reviewed and resolved or documented
```

---

## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED

These improve quality but can be deferred.

### Comment Density

```markdown
[ ] Appropriate level of documentation
    - Public APIs have documentation
    - Complex algorithms explained
    - Not over-commented (target <= 3 comments per 10 LOC)
```

### Code Organization

```markdown
[ ] Code is organized into logical sections
    - Related functionality grouped
    - Clear separation of concerns
    - Consistent ordering (constants, functions, exports)
```

### Line Length

```markdown
[ ] Reasonable line lengths maintained
    - Target: 80-100 characters
    - Maximum: 120 characters
    - Long lines broken at logical points
```

---

## 5. VALIDATION WORKFLOW

### Before Committing

```markdown
1. Run language-specific linter/type checker
   - JavaScript: ESLint or built-in checks
   - TypeScript: tsc --noEmit (type check without emitting)
   - Python: Black/flake8/mypy
   - Shell: ShellCheck

2. Run comment-hygiene checker on each modified file
   - .opencode/skills/sk-code/scripts/check-comment-hygiene.sh <file>
   - Zero violations required
   - Escape: add `// hygiene-ok` to suppress a specific line

3. Check this universal checklist
   - All P0 items must pass
   - P1 items addressed or approved

4. Run language-specific checklist
   - javascript_checklist.md
   - typescript_checklist.md
   - python_checklist.md
   - shell_checklist.md
   - config_checklist.md

5. Self-review
   - Would a colleague understand this?
   - Is anything overly clever?
   - Are edge cases handled?
```

### Quick Validation Commands

```bash
# JavaScript (ESLint if available)
npx eslint src/

# TypeScript (type check without emitting)
tsc --noEmit

# Python
python -m flake8 scripts/
python -m mypy scripts/

# Shell
shellcheck *.sh

# Config (JSON validation)
python -m json.tool config.json
```

---

## 6. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE

For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as cross-language surface evidence.

```markdown
## Cross-Language Standards Surface Evidence

- [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
- [ ] Language checklist validated for changed files
- [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
- [ ] Baseline risk checks sourced from `sk-code-review` references
- [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
```

---

## 7. RELATED RESOURCES

### Language-Specific Checklists
- [javascript_checklist.md](./javascript_checklist.md)
- [typescript_checklist.md](./typescript_checklist.md)
- [python_checklist.md](./python_checklist.md)
- [shell_checklist.md](./shell_checklist.md)
- [config_checklist.md](./config_checklist.md)

### Style Guides
- [JavaScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/javascript/style_guide.md)
- [TypeScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/typescript/style_guide.md)
- [Python Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/python/style_guide.md)
- [Shell Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/shell/style_guide.md)
- [Config Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/config/style_guide.md)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // DURABILITY: daemon re-election RELEASE-vs-KILL (real OS-level integration)
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: //
5: // Validates the OS-level contract the re-election feature depends on: with the
6: // flag ON, when the OWNER disposes (SIGTERM) it RELEASES the detached daemon
7: // (leaves it alive for a secondary to adopt); with the flag OFF it KILLS the
8: // daemon. It uses the REAL exported production decision functions from the
9: // launcher — contextServerSpawnIo() and shouldReleaseDaemonForReelection() —
10: // driving a real detached child and real SIGTERM/reparent semantics.
11: //
12: // SAFETY: this never imports config, never opens any sqlite, and never reads
13: // or writes a lease file or the IPC socket. The launcher's lease/socket dir is
14: // hardcoded relative to its own script location with no env override, so spawning
15: // the real repo launcher would bridge to (or clobber) the LIVE owner. Instead a
16: // throwaway "owner" harness in an OS temp dir spawns a trivial sleeper "daemon"
17: // stand-in using the real spawn-io the feature produces. Only processes this test
18: // starts are signalled, tracked by pid, and force-reaped in teardown.
19: 
20: import { spawn, type ChildProcess } from 'node:child_process';
21: import { mkdtempSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
22: import { createRequire } from 'node:module';
23: import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';
24: import { dirname, join, resolve } from 'node:path';
25: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
26: 
27: import { afterAll, afterEach, beforeAll, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
28: 
29: const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
30: // stress_test/durability/<file> -> repo root is six levels up.
31: const repoRoot = resolve(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../../../..');
32: const launcherPath = join(repoRoot, '.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs');
33: 
34: // The real production primitives under test. require() is side-effect-free here:
35: // the launcher only runs main() under `require.main === module`, never on import.
36: const launcher = require(launcherPath) as {
37:   daemonReelectionEnabled: (env?: Record<string, string | undefined>) => boolean;
38:   contextServerSpawnIo: (enabled: boolean) => { detached: boolean; stdio: [string, string, string] };
39:   shouldReleaseDaemonForReelection: (a?: { enabled?: boolean; hasLiveDaemon?: boolean }) => boolean;
40: };
41: 
42: // A throwaway owner harness. It requires the REAL launcher, computes spawn-io and
43: // the release-vs-kill decision from the production functions, spawns a trivial
44: // long-lived "daemon" (a sleeper), prints its pid, and stays alive until SIGTERM.
45: // On SIGTERM it applies the real decision: release (don't kill) or kill.
46: const OWNER_HARNESS = String.raw`
47: 'use strict';
48: const { spawn } = require('node:child_process');
49: const launcher = require(process.env.LAUNCHER_PATH);
50: 
51: const enabled = launcher.daemonReelectionEnabled(process.env);
52: const io = launcher.contextServerSpawnIo(enabled);
53: 
54: // Trivial daemon stand-in: a sleeper that keeps its own event loop alive.
55: const daemon = spawn(process.execPath, ['-e', 'setInterval(() => {}, 2147483647)'], {
56:   detached: io.detached,
57:   stdio: io.stdio,
58: });
59: // Re-election only: don't keep the detached daemon tethered to the owner (mirrors the launcher's unref()).
60: if (enabled) daemon.unref();
61: 
62: // Keep the owner alive and "owning" until it is disposed, independent of the daemon ref.
63: const keepAlive = setInterval(() => {}, 2147483647);
64: 
65: let shuttingDown = false;
66: function shutdown() {
67:   if (shuttingDown) return;
68:   shuttingDown = true;
69:   clearInterval(keepAlive);
70:   const hasLiveDaemon = daemon.exitCode === null && daemon.signalCode === null && typeof daemon.pid === 'number';
71:   if (launcher.shouldReleaseDaemonForReelection({ enabled, hasLiveDaemon })) {
72:     // RELEASE: leave the detached daemon alive for a secondary to adopt; just exit.
73:     process.stdout.write('RELEASED\n', () => process.exit(0));
74:   } else {
75:     // KILL: tear the daemon down, matching the non-reelection shutdown path.
76:     try { daemon.kill('SIGTERM'); } catch (_) {}
77:     process.stdout.write('KILLED\n', () => process.exit(0));
78:   }
79: }
80: for (const s of ['SIGTERM', 'SIGINT']) process.on(s, shutdown);
81: 
82: // Announce readiness only once the daemon is live and handlers are installed.
83: process.stdout.write('DAEMON_PID:' + daemon.pid + '\n');
84: `;
85: 
86: const BOOT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000;
87: const DEATH_TIMEOUT_MS = 8_000;
88: const SURVIVAL_CONFIRM_MS = 2_000;
89: 
90: let workDir: string;
91: let harnessPath: string;
92: const trackedPids = new Set<number>();
93: 
94: function track(pid: number | undefined): void {
95:   if (typeof pid === 'number' && pid > 0) trackedPids.add(pid);
96: }
97: 
98: function isAlive(pid: number): boolean {
99:   try {
100:     process.kill(pid, 0);
101:     return true;
102:   } catch (err) {
103:     // EPERM = the process exists but we may not signal it -> still alive.
104:     return (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === 'EPERM';
105:   }
106: }
107: 
108: function delay(ms: number): Promise<void> {
109:   return new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));
110: }
111: 
112: async function waitForDeath(pid: number, timeoutMs: number): Promise<boolean> {
113:   const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
114:   while (Date.now() < deadline) {
115:     if (!isAlive(pid)) return true;
116:     await delay(50);
117:   }
118:   return !isAlive(pid);
119: }
120: 
121: function waitForOwnerExit(owner: ChildProcess, timeoutMs: number): Promise<void> {
122:   return new Promise((resolveExit, rejectExit) => {
123:     if (owner.exitCode !== null || owner.signalCode !== null) return resolveExit();
124:     const timer = setTimeout(() => rejectExit(new Error('owner did not exit in time')), timeoutMs);
125:     owner.once('exit', () => {
126:       clearTimeout(timer);
127:       resolveExit();
128:     });
129:   });
130: }
131: 
132: // Spawn the owner harness and resolve with { owner, daemonPid } once it prints DAEMON_PID.
133: function spawnOwner(enabled: boolean): Promise<{ owner: ChildProcess; daemonPid: number }> {
134:   const owner = spawn(process.execPath, [harnessPath], {
135:     cwd: workDir,
136:     env: {
137:       ...process.env,
138:       LAUNCHER_PATH: launcherPath,
139:       SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION: enabled ? '1' : '0',
140:     },
141:     stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
142:   });
143:   track(owner.pid);
144: 
145:   return new Promise((resolveSpawn, rejectSpawn) => {
146:     let stdout = '';
147:     let stderr = '';
148:     const timer = setTimeout(() => {
149:       rejectSpawn(new Error(`owner never reported DAEMON_PID in ${BOOT_TIMEOUT_MS}ms; stderr: ${stderr}`));
150:     }, BOOT_TIMEOUT_MS);
151: 
152:     owner.stdout!.setEncoding('utf8');
153:     owner.stderr!.setEncoding('utf8');
154:     owner.stderr!.on('data', (chunk) => { stderr += chunk; });
155:     owner.stdout!.on('data', (chunk) => {
156:       stdout += chunk;
157:       const m = stdout.match(/DAEMON_PID:(\d+)/);
158:       if (m) {
159:         clearTimeout(timer);
160:         const daemonPid = Number(m[1]);
161:         track(daemonPid);
162:         resolveSpawn({ owner, daemonPid });
163:       }
164:     });
165:     owner.once('error', (err) => { clearTimeout(timer); rejectSpawn(err); });
166:   });
167: }
168: 
169: beforeAll(() => {
170:   workDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'speckit-reelect-'));
171:   harnessPath = join(workDir, 'owner-harness.cjs');
172:   writeFileSync(harnessPath, OWNER_HARNESS, 'utf8');
173: });
174: 
175: afterEach(async () => {
176:   // Force-reap ONLY processes this test started (owner + daemon stand-in). The
177:   // detached flag-ON daemon has reparented to init, so SIGKILL by pid is required.
178:   for (const pid of trackedPids) {
179:     try { process.kill(pid, 'SIGKILL'); } catch (_) { /* already gone */ }
180:   }
181:   // Give the OS a moment to release the reaped pids before the next case.
182:   await delay(100);
183:   trackedPids.clear();
184: });
185: 
186: afterAll(() => {
187:   rmSync(workDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
188: });
189: 
190: describe('daemon re-election: real release-vs-kill integration', () => {
191:   it('uses the real exported production primitives (not a mock)', () => {
192:     expect(typeof launcher.contextServerSpawnIo).toBe('function');
193:     expect(typeof launcher.shouldReleaseDaemonForReelection).toBe('function');
194:     expect(launcher.daemonReelectionEnabled({ SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION: '1' })).toBe(true);
195:     expect(launcher.contextServerSpawnIo(true)).toEqual({ detached: true, stdio: ['ignore', 'ignore', 'ignore'] });
196:     expect(launcher.contextServerSpawnIo(false)).toEqual({ detached: false, stdio: ['ignore', 'ignore', 'inherit'] });
197:   });
198: 
199:   it('flag ON: owner SIGTERM RELEASES the detached daemon (it survives)', async () => {
200:     const { owner, daemonPid } = await spawnOwner(true);
201:     expect(isAlive(daemonPid)).toBe(true);
202: 
203:     owner.kill('SIGTERM');
204:     await waitForOwnerExit(owner, DEATH_TIMEOUT_MS);
205: 
206:     // The released daemon must outlive its owner. Confirm it stays alive across a window.
207:     await delay(SURVIVAL_CONFIRM_MS);
208:     expect(isAlive(daemonPid)).toBe(true);
209:     expect(isAlive(owner.pid!)).toBe(false);
210:   });
211: 
212:   it('flag OFF: owner SIGTERM KILLS the daemon (it dies)', async () => {
213:     const { owner, daemonPid } = await spawnOwner(false);
214:     expect(isAlive(daemonPid)).toBe(true);
215: 
216:     owner.kill('SIGTERM');
217:     await waitForOwnerExit(owner, DEATH_TIMEOUT_MS);
218: 
219:     const died = await waitForDeath(daemonPid, DEATH_TIMEOUT_MS);
220:     expect(died).toBe(true);
221:     expect(isAlive(owner.pid!)).toBe(false);
222:   });
223: });

(End of file - total 223 lines)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // DURABILITY: daemon re-election LIVE two-session ADOPTION (real launchers)
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: //
5: // The sibling release-integration test proves the OS-level release-vs-kill
6: // DECISION with a sleeper stand-in. This test proves the END-TO-END behavior the
7: // feature actually promises: with the flag ON, when an OWNER session disposes the
8: // shared daemon SURVIVES and a SECONDARY session keeps its MCP transport; with the
9: // flag OFF the daemon DIES with its owner and the secondary loses transport.
10: //
11: // It runs TWO real mk-spec-memory launchers as MCP stdio servers against ONE
12: // isolated fake-root, speaks real MCP JSON-RPC to each, disposes the owner with
13: // SIGTERM (what a host does on session end), and observes the shared daemon's pid
14: // and the secondary's next call.
15: //
16: // SAFETY: everything runs in a throwaway fake-root under the OS temp dir. The
17: // launcher is REAL-COPIED (so its __dirname-derived lease/db dir is isolated) and
18: // the daemon's dist is REAL-COPIED (so the daemon's workspace-root/DB boundary
19: // guard resolves to the fake root, never the production tree); node_modules and
20: // scripts are symlinked; the IPC socket lives in a short isolated dir. No
21: // production lease, DB, or socket is read or written. Only processes this test
22: // starts are signalled, tracked by pid, and force-reaped in teardown.
23: 
24: import { spawn, spawnSync, type ChildProcess } from 'node:child_process';
25: import { cpSync, copyFileSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, symlinkSync } from 'node:fs';
26: import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';
27: import { dirname, join, resolve } from 'node:path';
28: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
29: 
30: import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
31: 
32: // stress_test/durability/<file> -> repo root is six levels up.
33: const repoRoot = resolve(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../../../..');
34: const realBin = join(repoRoot, '.opencode/bin');
35: const realKit = join(repoRoot, '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit');
36: const realMcp = join(realKit, 'mcp_server');
37: 
38: const trackedPids = new Set<number>();
39: const tempDirs = new Set<string>();
40: 
41: function track(pid: number | undefined): void {
42:   if (typeof pid === 'number' && pid > 0) trackedPids.add(pid);
43: }
44: function isAlive(pid: number): boolean {
45:   try { process.kill(pid, 0); return true; }
46:   catch (e) { return (e as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === 'EPERM'; }
47: }
48: function delay(ms: number): Promise<void> { return new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms)); }
49: 
50: // spawnSync (no shell) so the temp-dir argument is never interpreted by a shell. pgrep exits
51: // non-zero with empty stdout when nothing matches, which yields an empty list.
52: function pgrepContains(needle: string): number[] {
53:   const res = spawnSync('pgrep', ['-f', needle], { encoding: 'utf8' });
54:   const out = (res.stdout || '').trim();
55:   return out ? out.split('\n').map(Number).filter(Boolean) : [];
56: }
57: 
58: // Distinct pids holding any *.sqlite* file open under dbDir. One = single writer. spawnSync (no
59: // shell) plus JS parsing, so dbDir is never interpolated into a shell command. We parse stdout
60: // regardless of exit status because `lsof +D` often exits non-zero on benign warnings while still
61: // listing the open files.
62: function sqliteOpenerPids(dbDir: string): number[] {
63:   const res = spawnSync('lsof', ['+D', dbDir], { encoding: 'utf8' });
64:   const out = res.stdout || '';
65:   const pids = new Set<number>();
66:   for (const line of out.split('\n').slice(1)) {
67:     const cols = line.split(/\s+/);
68:     const name = cols[8] || '';
69:     if (/\.(db|sqlite|sqlite3)/.test(name)) {
70:       const pid = Number(cols[1]);
71:       if (Number.isInteger(pid) && pid > 0) pids.add(pid);
72:     }
73:   }
74:   return [...pids];
75: }
76: 
77: // Build an isolated fake-root: real-copied launcher + daemon dist, symlinked deps,
78: // fresh lease/db dir. Returns the paths the two sessions share.
79: function buildFakeRoot(): { base: string; sockDir: string; dbDir: string; launcher: string } {
80:   const base = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'rladopt.'));
81:   tempDirs.add(base);
82:   const fbin = join(base, '.opencode/bin');
83:   const fkit = join(base, '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit');
84:   const fmcp = join(fkit, 'mcp_server');
85:   mkdirSync(fbin, { recursive: true });
86:   mkdirSync(fmcp, { recursive: true });
87:   copyFileSync(join(realBin, 'mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs'), join(fbin, 'mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs'));
88:   symlinkSync(join(realBin, 'lib'), join(fbin, 'lib'));
89:   cpSync(join(realMcp, 'dist'), join(fmcp, 'dist'), { recursive: true });
90:   symlinkSync(join(realMcp, 'node_modules'), join(fmcp, 'node_modules'));
91:   if (existsSync(join(realKit, 'node_modules'))) symlinkSync(join(realKit, 'node_modules'), join(fkit, 'node_modules'));
92:   symlinkSync(join(realMcp, 'package.json'), join(fmcp, 'package.json'));
93:   symlinkSync(join(realKit, 'scripts'), join(fkit, 'scripts'));
94:   const dbDir = join(fmcp, 'database');
95:   mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
96:   const sockDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'rladsk.'));
97:   tempDirs.add(sockDir);
98:   return { base, sockDir, dbDir, launcher: join(fbin, 'mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs') };
99: }
100: 
101: type RpcResponse = { result?: unknown; error?: unknown; id?: number | null };
102: 
103: // A minimal newline-delimited MCP JSON-RPC client over a launcher's stdio.
104: function makeClient(child: ChildProcess) {
105:   let buf = '';
106:   const pending = new Map<number, (m: RpcResponse) => void>();
107:   child.stdout!.setEncoding('utf8');
108:   child.stdout!.on('data', (chunk: string) => {
109:     buf += chunk;
110:     let nl: number;
111:     while ((nl = buf.indexOf('\n')) >= 0) {
112:       const line = buf.slice(0, nl).trim();
113:       buf = buf.slice(nl + 1);
114:       if (!line) continue;
115:       let msg: RpcResponse;
116:       try { msg = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
117:       if (msg.id != null && pending.has(msg.id)) { pending.get(msg.id)!(msg); pending.delete(msg.id); }
118:     }
119:   });
120:   child.stderr!.setEncoding('utf8');
121:   child.stderr!.on('data', () => { /* swallow launcher stderr */ });
122:   let nextId = 1;
123:   function request(method: string, params: unknown, timeoutMs = 30_000): Promise<RpcResponse> {
124:     const id = nextId++;
125:     return new Promise((resolveReq, rejectReq) => {
126:       const timer = setTimeout(() => { pending.delete(id); rejectReq(new Error(`${method} timed out`)); }, timeoutMs);
127:       pending.set(id, (m) => { clearTimeout(timer); resolveReq(m); });
128:       try { child.stdin!.write(JSON.stringify({ jsonrpc: '2.0', id, method, params }) + '\n'); }
129:       catch (e) { clearTimeout(timer); pending.delete(id); rejectReq(e as Error); }
130:     });
131:   }
132:   function notify(method: string, params: unknown): void {
133:     try { child.stdin!.write(JSON.stringify({ jsonrpc: '2.0', method, params }) + '\n'); } catch { /* gone */ }
134:   }
135:   return { request, notify };
136: }
137: 
138: async function startSession(
139:   paths: { base: string; sockDir: string; dbDir: string; launcher: string },
140:   enabled: boolean,
141: ): Promise<{ child: ChildProcess; client: ReturnType<typeof makeClient> }> {
142:   const child = spawn(process.execPath, [paths.launcher], {
143:     cwd: paths.base,
144:     env: {
145:       ...process.env,
146:       SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION: enabled ? '1' : '0',
147:       SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR: paths.sockDir,
148:       SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR: paths.dbDir,
149:       SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_LOG: '1',
150:       AI_SESSION_CHILD: '1',
151:     },
152:     stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
153:   });
154:   track(child.pid);
155:   const client = makeClient(child);
156:   const init = await client.request('initialize', {
157:     protocolVersion: '2024-11-05', capabilities: {}, clientInfo: { name: 'reelect-adopt', version: '1.0' },
158:   }, 30_000);
159:   if (!init.result) throw new Error(`initialize failed: ${JSON.stringify(init.error)}`);
160:   client.notify('notifications/initialized', {});
161:   return { child, client };
162: }
163: 
164: function leaseDaemonPid(base: string): number | null {
165:   const f = join(base, '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.mk-spec-memory-launcher.json');
166:   try { return (JSON.parse(readFileSync(f, 'utf8')).childPid as number) ?? null; } catch { return null; }
167: }
168: async function statsOk(client: ReturnType<typeof makeClient>, timeoutMs = 15_000): Promise<boolean> {
169:   try {
170:     const r = await client.request('tools/call', { name: 'memory_stats', arguments: {} }, timeoutMs);
171:     return !r.error;
172:   } catch { return false; }
173: }
174: async function waitForExit(pid: number, timeoutMs: number): Promise<void> {
175:   const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
176:   while (isAlive(pid) && Date.now() < deadline) await delay(50);
177: }
178: 
179: afterEach(async () => {
180:   for (const pid of trackedPids) { try { process.kill(pid, 'SIGKILL'); } catch { /* gone */ } }
181:   await delay(150);
182:   // Sweep any detached daemon / embed sidecar that reparented to init.
183:   for (const dir of tempDirs) {
184:     for (const pid of pgrepContains(dir)) { try { process.kill(pid, 'SIGKILL'); } catch { /* gone */ } }
185:   }
186:   await delay(150);
187:   trackedPids.clear();
188:   for (const dir of tempDirs) { try { rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true }); } catch { /* best effort */ } }
189:   tempDirs.clear();
190: });
191: 
192: describe('daemon re-election: live two-session adoption', () => {
193:   it('flag ON: owner disposal RELEASES the shared daemon; secondary keeps transport', async () => {
194:     const paths = buildFakeRoot();
195: 
196:     const owner = await startSession(paths, true);
197:     expect(await statsOk(owner.client)).toBe(true);
198:     const daemonPid = leaseDaemonPid(paths.base);
199:     expect(daemonPid).toBeTypeOf('number');
200:     track(daemonPid!);
201:     expect(isAlive(daemonPid!)).toBe(true);
202: 
203:     // Secondary joins while the owner holds the lease and the socket is live.
204:     const secondary = await startSession(paths, true);
205:     expect(await statsOk(secondary.client)).toBe(true);
206:     // The secondary must BRIDGE to the owner's daemon, not spawn its own.
207:     expect(leaseDaemonPid(paths.base)).toBe(daemonPid);
208: 
209:     // Dispose the owner.
210:     owner.child.kill('SIGTERM');
211:     await waitForExit(owner.child.pid!, 8_000);
212:     expect(isAlive(owner.child.pid!)).toBe(false);
213:     await delay(1_200);
214: 
215:     // The released daemon outlives its owner and the secondary keeps transport.
216:     expect(isAlive(daemonPid!)).toBe(true);
217:     expect(await statsOk(secondary.client)).toBe(true);
218:   });
219: 
220:   it('flag ON: a FRESH session after owner disposal ADOPTS the released daemon (single writer)', async () => {
221:     const paths = buildFakeRoot();
222: 
223:     const owner = await startSession(paths, true);
224:     expect(await statsOk(owner.client)).toBe(true);
225:     const orphanPid = leaseDaemonPid(paths.base);
226:     expect(orphanPid).toBeTypeOf('number');
227:     track(orphanPid!);
228: 
229:     // Dispose the owner: the daemon is RELEASED (survives), not killed.
230:     owner.child.kill('SIGTERM');
231:     await waitForExit(owner.child.pid!, 8_000);
232:     await delay(1_200);
233:     expect(isAlive(orphanPid!)).toBe(true);
234: 
235:     // A brand-new session starts AFTER disposal. With true adoption it bridges to the still-live
236:     // released daemon instead of reaping and respawning, so the warm daemon is reused and there is
237:     // never a second writer.
238:     const fresh = await startSession(paths, true);
239:     expect(await statsOk(fresh.client)).toBe(true);
240:     const newPid = leaseDaemonPid(paths.base);
241:     expect(newPid).toBeTypeOf('number');
242:     track(newPid!);
243:     await delay(800);
244: 
245:     // Adoption invariant: the released daemon is reused (same pid, still alive), not respawned.
246:     expect(isAlive(orphanPid!)).toBe(true);
247:     expect(newPid).toBe(orphanPid);
248: 
249:     // Airtight co-residency check: exactly one pid holds the sqlite open under the DB dir.
250:     const dbWriters = sqliteOpenerPids(paths.dbDir);
251:     expect(dbWriters.length).toBe(1);
252:   });
253: 
254:   it('flag OFF: owner disposal KILLS the shared daemon; secondary loses transport', async () => {
255:     const paths = buildFakeRoot();
256: 
257:     const owner = await startSession(paths, false);
258:     expect(await statsOk(owner.client)).toBe(true);
259:     const daemonPid = leaseDaemonPid(paths.base);
260:     expect(daemonPid).toBeTypeOf('number');
261:     track(daemonPid!);
262:     expect(isAlive(daemonPid!)).toBe(true);
263: 
264:     const secondary = await startSession(paths, false);
265:     expect(await statsOk(secondary.client)).toBe(true);
266:     expect(leaseDaemonPid(paths.base)).toBe(daemonPid);
267: 
268:     owner.child.kill('SIGTERM');
269:     await waitForExit(owner.child.pid!, 8_000);
270:     expect(isAlive(owner.child.pid!)).toBe(false);
271:     await delay(1_200);
272: 
273:     // With the flag off, the daemon dies with its owner and the secondary's next
274:     // call fails (no surviving daemon to serve it) within a short window.
275:     expect(isAlive(daemonPid!)).toBe(false);
276:     expect(await statsOk(secondary.client, 6_000)).toBe(false);
277:   });
