1: // MODULE: Launcher Idle Timeout Monitor
2: 
3: const DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN = 30;
4: const MS_PER_MINUTE = 60_000;
5: const MIN_IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS = 1_000;
6: const MAX_IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS = 60_000;
7: 
8: type IntervalHandle = ReturnType<typeof setInterval>;
9: type DataListener = (chunk: unknown) => void;
10: type StdinLike = {
11:   on(eventName: 'data', listener: DataListener): unknown;
12:   off?: (eventName: 'data', listener: DataListener) => unknown;
13:   removeListener?: (eventName: 'data', listener: DataListener) => unknown;
14: };
15: 
16: interface LauncherIdleMonitorOptions {
17:   readonly serviceName: string;
18:   readonly timeoutMinutesRaw?: string;
19:   readonly stdin?: StdinLike | null;
20:   readonly getActiveClientCount?: () => number;
21:   readonly onIdle: () => void | Promise<void>;
22:   readonly log?: (message: string) => void;
23:   readonly now?: () => number;
24:   readonly setIntervalFn?: (callback: () => void, ms: number) => IntervalHandle;
25:   readonly clearIntervalFn?: (timer: IntervalHandle) => void;
26: }
27: 
28: interface LauncherIdleMonitor {
29:   readonly timeoutMs: number | null;
30:   readonly markActivity: () => void;
31:   readonly stop: () => void;
32: }
33: 
34: function parseLauncherIdleTimeoutMs(rawValue = process.env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN): number | null {
35:   const raw = rawValue === undefined || rawValue === null || String(rawValue).trim() === ''
36:     ? String(DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN)
37:     : String(rawValue).trim();
38:   const parsedMinutes = Number(raw);
39:   if (!Number.isFinite(parsedMinutes) || parsedMinutes < 0) {
40:     return DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN * MS_PER_MINUTE;
41:   }
42:   if (parsedMinutes === 0) {
43:     return null;
44:   }
45:   return Math.max(1, Math.round(parsedMinutes * MS_PER_MINUTE));
46: }
47: 
48: function removeDataListener(stdin: StdinLike | null | undefined, listener: DataListener): void {
49:   if (!stdin) return;
50:   if (typeof stdin.off === 'function') {
51:     stdin.off('data', listener);
52:     return;
53:   }
54:   if (typeof stdin.removeListener === 'function') {
55:     stdin.removeListener('data', listener);
56:   }
57: }
58: 
59: function createLauncherIdleMonitor(options: LauncherIdleMonitorOptions): LauncherIdleMonitor {
60:   const log = options.log ?? ((message: string) => console.error(message));
61:   const timeoutMs = parseLauncherIdleTimeoutMs(options.timeoutMinutesRaw);
62:   const noop = () => undefined;
63:   if (timeoutMs === null) {
64:     log(`[${options.serviceName}] launcher idle timeout disabled`);
65:     return { timeoutMs, markActivity: noop, stop: noop };
66:   }
67: 
68:   const now = options.now ?? (() => Date.now());
69:   const setIntervalFn = options.setIntervalFn ?? ((callback: () => void, ms: number) => setInterval(callback, ms));
70:   const clearIntervalFn = options.clearIntervalFn ?? ((interval: IntervalHandle) => clearInterval(interval));
71:   const stdin = options.stdin === undefined ? process.stdin : options.stdin;
72:   const checkIntervalMs = Math.min(
73:     MAX_IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS,
74:     Math.max(MIN_IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS, Math.floor(timeoutMs / 2)),
75:   );
76: 
77:   let stopped = false;
78:   let lastActivityAt = now();
79:   let timer: IntervalHandle | null = null;
80:   let sawActiveClient = false;
81:   let zeroActiveClientChecks = 0;
82: 
83:   const markActivity = () => {
84:     if (!stopped) {
85:       lastActivityAt = now();
86:     }
87:   };
88: 
89:   const stop = () => {
90:     if (stopped) return;
91:     stopped = true;
92:     removeDataListener(stdin, markActivity);
93:     if (timer !== null) {
94:       clearIntervalFn(timer);
95:     }
96:   };
97: 
98:   const checkIdle = async () => {
99:     if (stopped) return;
100:     const activeClients = options.getActiveClientCount?.() ?? 0;
101:     if (activeClients > 0) {
102:       sawActiveClient = true;
103:       zeroActiveClientChecks = 0;
104:       lastActivityAt = now();
105:       return;
106:     }
107:     zeroActiveClientChecks += 1;
108:     if (sawActiveClient && zeroActiveClientChecks < 2) {
109:       // A reconnect can briefly drop the socket count to zero while the launcher is still preserving stdio.
110:       lastActivityAt = now();
111:       return;
112:     }
113:     const idleForMs = now() - lastActivityAt;
114:     if (idleForMs < timeoutMs) return;
115: 
116:     stopped = true;
117:     removeDataListener(stdin, markActivity);
118:     if (timer !== null) {
119:       clearIntervalFn(timer);
120:     }
121:     log(`[${options.serviceName}] launcher idle timeout reached after ${Math.round(idleForMs)}ms`);
122:     try {
123:       await options.onIdle();
124:     } catch (error: unknown) {
125:       const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
126:       log(`[${options.serviceName}] launcher idle shutdown failed: ${message}`);
127:     }
128:   };
129: 
130:   stdin?.on('data', markActivity);
131:   timer = setIntervalFn(() => {
132:     void checkIdle();
133:   }, checkIntervalMs);
134:   if (typeof (timer as { unref?: () => void }).unref === 'function') {
135:     (timer as { unref: () => void }).unref();
136:   }
137: 
138:   return { timeoutMs, markActivity, stop };
139: }
140: 
141: export {
142:   createLauncherIdleMonitor,
143:   parseLauncherIdleTimeoutMs,
144: };
145: 
146: export type {
147:   LauncherIdleMonitor,
148:   LauncherIdleMonitorOptions,
149: };

<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Clean up MCP helper descendants owned by the current Claude Code session.
3: #
4: # This script is intentionally session-scoped: it only ever kills processes it
5: # can prove are descendants of CLAUDE_SESSION_PID, and it re-proves that
6: # ancestry immediately before each kill. There is deliberately NO fallback to
7: # the hook's PPID — under a shared terminal that PPID can resolve to an
8: # ancestor common to MANY live sessions, turning "this session's descendants"
9: # into "every session's MCP helpers" and killing sibling sessions' transports
10: # mid-flight. Missing session identity means we do nothing.
11: 
12: set -euo pipefail
13: 
14: SESSION_PID="${CLAUDE_SESSION_PID:-}"
15: LOG_PATH="${CLAUDE_SESSION_CLEANUP_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/claude-stop-hook.log}"
16: EXIT_CODE=0
17: descendants=()
18: 
19: # Orphan-sweep fallback for the no-session-pid case. Default off. "dry-run" logs candidate reaps
20: # without mutating; "1"/"on"/"live" reaps. It delegates to the ORPHAN-ONLY sweeper, which reaps only
21: # ownerless (reparented) MCP processes and so can never touch a live sibling session — unlike a PPID
22: # guess, which this script deliberately refuses (see header). The sweeper path is overridable for tests.
23: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
24: ORPHAN_SWEEP_MODE="${SPECKIT_STOP_HOOK_ORPHAN_SWEEP:-off}"
25: ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN="${SPECKIT_ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN:-$SCRIPT_DIR/orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh}"
26: 
27: emit() {
28:   local line
29:   line="$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z') $*"
30:   printf '%s\n' "$line"
31:   if [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
32:     mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_PATH")" 2>/dev/null || true
33:     printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true
34:   fi
35: }
36: 
37: pid_in_descendants() {
38:   local needle="$1"
39:   local item
40:   [ "${#descendants[@]}" -gt 0 ] || return 1
41:   for item in "${descendants[@]}"; do
42:     [ "$item" = "$needle" ] && return 0
43:   done
44:   return 1
45: }
46: 
47: append_descendants() {
48:   local parent="$1"
49:   local child
50:   while read -r child; do
51:     [ -n "$child" ] || continue
52:     if ! pid_in_descendants "$child"; then
53:       descendants+=("$child")
54:       append_descendants "$child"
55:     fi
56:   done <<EOF
57: $(pgrep -P "$parent" 2>/dev/null || true)
58: EOF
59: }
60: 
61: is_target_command() {
62:   local cmd="$1"
63:   case "$cmd" in
64:     *"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"*|\
65:     *"mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"*|\
66:     *"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"*|\
67:     *"system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"*|\
68:     *"system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/"*"advisor-server.js"*|\
69:     *"system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*|\
70:     *"mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*|\
71:     *"@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*|\
72:     *"server-sequential-thinking"*|\
73:     *"clickup-mcp-server"*)
74:       return 0
75:       ;;
76:   esac
77:   return 1
78: }
79: 
80: # Re-prove, at kill time, that a candidate is still a descendant of the
81: # session. The pgrep walk above is a snapshot; a process can be re-parented
82: # (orphan adoption by launchd/init) between the walk and the kill, and a pid
83: # can in principle be recycled. Walking the live ppid chain upward closes
84: # that race: a process belonging to a sibling session can never have OUR
85: # session pid in its ancestor chain.
86: is_descendant_of_session() {
87:   local pid="$1"
88:   local depth=0
89:   local current="$pid"
90:   while [ "$depth" -lt 15 ]; do
91:     current="$(ps -o ppid= -p "$current" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)"
92:     [ -n "$current" ] || return 1
93:     [ "$current" = "0" ] && return 1
94:     [ "$current" = "1" ] && return 1
95:     if [ "$current" = "$SESSION_PID" ]; then
96:       return 0
97:     fi
98:     depth=$((depth + 1))
99:   done
100:   return 1
101: }
102: 
103: # Without a session pid the session-scoped kill below is impossible, and guessing is unsafe. Fall
104: # back to the orphan-only sweeper when explicitly enabled; otherwise keep the historical no-op.
105: run_orphan_sweep_fallback() {
106:   case "$ORPHAN_SWEEP_MODE" in
107:     1|on|live)
108:       emit "action=orphan-sweep mode=live reason=no-session-pid"
109:       bash "$ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN" || true
110:       ;;
111:     dry-run|dryrun|dry)
112:       emit "action=orphan-sweep mode=dry-run reason=no-session-pid"
113:       bash "$ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN" --dry-run || true
114:       ;;
115:     *)
116:       emit "action=skip reason=no-session-pid"
117:       ;;
118:   esac
119: }
120: 
121: if [ -z "$SESSION_PID" ]; then
122:   run_orphan_sweep_fallback
123:   exit 0
124: fi
125: if ! kill -0 "$SESSION_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
126:   emit "action=skip reason=session-pid-not-alive session_pid=$SESSION_PID"
127:   exit 0
128: fi
129: 
130: append_descendants "$SESSION_PID"
131: emit "action=start session_pid=$SESSION_PID descendants=${#descendants[@]}"
132: 
133: if [ "${#descendants[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
134:   for pid in "${descendants[@]}"; do
135:     cmd="$(ps -p "$pid" -o command= 2>/dev/null || true)"
136:     [ -n "$cmd" ] || continue
137:     if is_target_command "$cmd"; then
138:       if ! is_descendant_of_session "$pid"; then
139:         emit "action=skip-kill reason=ancestry-not-confirmed pid=$pid matched_by=name ancestor_ok=no command=$cmd"
140:         continue
141:       fi
142:       emit "action=kill signal=TERM pid=$pid matched_by=name ancestor_ok=yes command=$cmd"
143:       if ! kill -15 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
144:         emit "action=kill-failed signal=TERM pid=$pid"
145:         EXIT_CODE=1
146:       fi
147:     fi
148:   done
149: fi
150: 
151: emit "action=summary session_pid=$SESSION_PID exit_code=$EXIT_CODE"
152: exit "$EXIT_CODE"

(End of file - total 152 lines)
</content>

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Clean up MCP helper descendants owned by the current Claude Code session.
#
# This script is intentionally session-scoped: it only ever kills processes it
# can prove are descendants of CLAUDE_SESSION_PID, and it re-proves that
# ancestry immediately before each kill. There is deliberately NO fallback to
# the hook's PPID — under a shared terminal that PPID can resolve to an

# Orphan-sweep fallback for the no-session-pid case. Default off. "dry-run" logs candidate reaps

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Clean up MCP helper descendants owned by the current Claude Code session.
#
# This script is intentionally session-scoped: it only ever kills processes it
# can prove are descendants of CLAUDE_SESSION_PID, and it re-proves that
# ancestry immediately before each kill. There is deliberately NO fallback to
# the hook's PPID — under a shared terminal that PPID can resolve to an
# ancestor common to MANY live sessions, turning "this session's descendants"
# into "every session's MCP helpers" and killing sibling sessions' transports
# mid-flight. Missing session identity means we do nothing.

set -euo pipefail

SESSION_PID="${CLAUDE_SESSION_PID:-}"
LOG_PATH="${CLAUDE_SESSION_CLEANUP_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/claude-stop-hook.log}"
EXIT_CODE=0
descendants=()

# Orphan-sweep fallback for the no-session-pid case. Default off. "dry-run" logs candidate reaps
# without mutating; "1"/"on"/"live" reaps. It delegates to the ORPHAN-ONLY sweeper, which reaps only
# ownerless (reparented) MCP processes and so can never touch a live sibling session — unlike a PPID
# guess, which this script deliberately refuses (see header). The sweeper path is overridable for tests.
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ORPHAN_SWEEP_MODE="${SPECKIT_STOP_HOOK_ORPHAN_SWEEP:-off}"
ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN="${SPECKIT_ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN:-$SCRIPT_DIR/orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh}"

emit() {
  local line
  line="$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z') $*"
  printf '%s\n' "$line"
  if [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_PATH")" 2>/dev/null || true
    printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}

pid_in_descendants() {
  local needle="$1"
  local item
  [ "${#descendants[@]}" -gt 0 ] || return 1
  for item in "${descendants[@]}"; do
    [ "$item" = "$needle" ] && return 0
  done
  return 1
}

append_descendants() {
  local parent="$1"
  local child
  while read -r child; do
    [ -n "$child" ] || continue
    if ! pid_in_descendants "$child"; then
      descendants+=("$child")
      append_descendants "$child"
    fi
  done <<EOF
$(pgrep -P "$parent" 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
}

is_target_command() {
  local cmd="$1"
  case "$cmd" in
    *"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"*|\
    *"mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"*|\
    *"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"*|\
    *"system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"*|\
    *"system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/"*"advisor-server.js"*|\
    *"system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*|\
    *"mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*|\
    *"@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*|\
    *"server-sequential-thinking"*|\
    *"clickup-mcp-server"*)
      return 0
      ;;
  esac
  return 1
}

# Re-prove, at kill time, that a candidate is still a descendant of the
# session. The pgrep walk above is a snapshot; a process can be re-parented
# (orphan adoption by launchd/init) between the walk and the kill, and a pid
# can in principle be recycled. Walking the live ppid chain upward closes
# that race: a process belonging to a sibling session can never have OUR
# session pid in its ancestor chain.
is_descendant_of_session() {
  local pid="$1"
  local depth=0
  local current="$pid"
  while [ "$depth" -lt 15 ]; do
    current="$(ps -o ppid= -p "$current" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)"
    [ -n "$current" ] || return 1
    [ "$current" = "0" ] && return 1
    [ "$current" = "1" ] && return 1
    if [ "$current" = "$SESSION_PID" ]; then
      return 0
    fi
    depth=$((depth + 1))
  done
  return 1
}

# Without a session pid the session-scoped kill below is impossible, and guessing is unsafe. Fall
# back to the orphan-only sweeper when explicitly enabled; otherwise keep the historical no-op.
run_orphan_sweep_fallback() {
  case "$ORPHAN_SWEEP_MODE" in
    1|on|live)
      emit "action=orphan-sweep mode=live reason=no-session-pid"
      bash "$ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN" || true
      ;;
    dry-run|dryrun|dry)
      emit "action=orphan-sweep mode=dry-run reason=no-session-pid"
      bash "$ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN" --dry-run || true
      ;;
    *)
      emit "action=skip reason=no-session-pid"
      ;;
  esac
}

if [ -z "$SESSION_PID" ]; then
  run_orphan_sweep_fallback
  exit 0
fi
if ! kill -0 "$SESSION_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
  emit "action=skip reason=session-pid-not-alive session_pid=$SESSION_PID"
  exit 0
fi

append_descendants "$SESSION_PID"
emit "action=start session_pid=$SESSION_PID descendants=${#descendants[@]}"

if [ "${#descendants[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
  for pid in "${descendants[@]}"; do
    cmd="$(ps -p "$pid" -o command= 2>/dev/null || true)"
    [ -n "$cmd" ] || continue
    if is_target_command "$cmd"; then
      if ! is_descendant_of_session "$pid"; then
        emit "action=skip-kill reason=ancestry-not-confirmed pid=$pid matched_by=name ancestor_ok=no command=$cmd"
        continue
      fi
      emit "action=kill signal=TERM pid=$pid matched_by=name ancestor_ok=yes command=$cmd"
      if ! kill -15 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        emit "action=kill-failed signal=TERM pid=$pid"
        EXIT_CODE=1
      fi
    fi
  done
fi

emit "action=summary session_pid=$SESSION_PID exit_code=$EXIT_CODE"
exit "$EXIT_CODE"

6: #
7: # Defaults:
8: #   ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC=300
9: #   ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
10: #   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
11: #   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH=$HOME/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log

18: TMP_AGE_HOURS="${ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS:-24}"
19: LOG_PATH="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log}"
20: LOG_EXPLICIT=false
21: LOG_MAX_BYTES="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES:-10485760}"
22: EXIT_CODE=0
23:

46:   cat <<'EOF'
47: Usage: orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
48: 
49: Options:
50:   --dry-run        Log matching kill/remove actions without mutating anything.
51:   --verbose        Log preserve decisions as well as actions.
52:   --log-path PATH  Append logs to PATH. Default: ~/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log.
53:   -h, --help       Show this help.
54: EOF
55: }
56: 
57: while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
58:   case "$1" in
59:     --dry-run)
60:       DRY_RUN=true
61:       ;;
62:     --verbose)
63:       VERBOSE=true
64:       ;;
65:     --log-path)

70:       LOG_PATH="$2"
71:       LOG_EXPLICIT=true
72:       shift
73:       ;;

76:       exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweep stale MCP helper processes and temporary dispatch artifacts.
#
# Usage:
#   orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
#
# Defaults:
#   ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC=300
#   ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH=$HOME/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log

set -euo pipefail

DRY_RUN=false
VERBOSE=false
AGE_MIN_SEC="${ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC:-300}"
TMP_AGE_HOURS="${ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS:-24}"
LOG_PATH="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log}"
LOG_EXPLICIT=false

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweep stale MCP helper processes and temporary dispatch artifacts.
#
# Usage:
#   orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
#
# Defaults:
#   ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC=300
#   ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH=$HOME/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log

set -euo pipefail

DRY_RUN=false
VERBOSE=false
AGE_MIN_SEC="${ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC:-300}"
TMP_AGE_HOURS="${ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS:-24}"
LOG_PATH="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log}"
LOG_EXPLICIT=false
LOG_MAX_BYTES="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES:-10485760}"
EXIT_CODE=0

candidate_pids=()
candidate_ppids=()
candidate_classes=()
candidate_ages=()
candidate_etimes=()
candidate_rsses=()
candidate_commands=()

kill_pids=()
kill_classes=()
kill_ages=()
kill_etimes=()
kill_rsses=()
kill_commands=()

min_classes=()
min_ages=()
min_pids=()

session_tree_pids=()

usage() {
  cat <<'EOF'
Usage: orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]

Options:
  --dry-run        Log matching kill/remove actions without mutating anything.
  --verbose        Log preserve decisions as well as actions.
  --log-path PATH  Append logs to PATH. Default: ~/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log.
  -h, --help       Show this help.
EOF
}

while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=true
      ;;
    --verbose)
      VERBOSE=true
      ;;
    --log-path)
      if [ "$#" -lt 2 ]; then
        echo "ERROR: --log-path requires a path" >&2
        exit 1
      fi
      LOG_PATH="$2"
      LOG_EXPLICIT=true
      shift
      ;;
    -h|--help)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      echo "ERROR: unknown argument: $1" >&2
      usage >&2
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac
  shift
done

is_positive_int() {
  case "$1" in
    ''|*[!0-9]*) return 1 ;;
    *) [ "$1" -gt 0 ] ;;
  esac
}

if ! is_positive_int "$AGE_MIN_SEC"; then
  AGE_MIN_SEC=300
fi
if ! is_positive_int "$TMP_AGE_HOURS"; then
  TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
fi
if ! is_positive_int "$LOG_MAX_BYTES"; then
  LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
fi

should_append_log_file() {
  [ "$LOG_EXPLICIT" = true ] || [ -t 1 ]
}

rotate_log_if_needed() {
  should_append_log_file || return 0
  [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ] || return 0
  [ -f "$LOG_PATH" ] || return 0

  local size
  size="$(wc -c < "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  is_positive_int "$size" || size=0
  [ "$size" -le "$LOG_MAX_BYTES" ] && return 0

  mv -f "$LOG_PATH.2" "$LOG_PATH.3" 2>/dev/null || true
  mv -f "$LOG_PATH.1" "$LOG_PATH.2" 2>/dev/null || true
  mv -f "$LOG_PATH" "$LOG_PATH.1" 2>/dev/null || true
}

emit() {
  local line
  line="$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z') $*"
  printf '%s\n' "$line"
  if should_append_log_file && [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_PATH")" 2>/dev/null || true
    printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}

log_action() {
  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
    emit "[DRY-RUN] $*"
  else
    emit "$*"
  fi
}

log_verbose() {
  [ "$VERBOSE" = true ] || return 0
  emit "$*"
}

etime_to_seconds() {
  local raw="$1"
  local days=0
  local rest="$raw"
  local h=0
  local m=0
  local s=0
  local a b c

  case "$rest" in
    *-*)
      days="${rest%%-*}"
      rest="${rest#*-}"
      ;;
  esac

  IFS=: read -r a b c <<EOF
$rest
EOF
  if [ -n "${c:-}" ]; then
    h="$a"
    m="$b"
    s="$c"
  elif [ -n "${b:-}" ]; then
    m="$a"
    s="$b"
  else
    s="$a"
  fi

  days="${days#0}"; h="${h#0}"; m="${m#0}"; s="${s#0}"
  days="${days:-0}"; h="${h:-0}"; m="${m:-0}"; s="${s:-0}"
  printf '%s\n' $((days * 86400 + h * 3600 + m * 60 + s))
}

classify_command() {
  local cmd="$1"
  case "$cmd" in
    *"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-spec-memory-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-skill-advisor-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-code-index-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"*) printf '%s\n' "spec-memory-context-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/"*"advisor-server.js"*) printf '%s\n' "skill-advisor-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*) printf '%s\n' "code-graph-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*) printf '%s\n' "mcp-code-mode"; return 0 ;;
    *"npm exec @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server"*) printf '%s\n' "clickup-mcp-wrapper"; return 0 ;;
    *"clickup-mcp-server"*) printf '%s\n' "clickup-mcp-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"npm exec @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*)
      printf '%s\n' "sequential-thinking-wrapper"
      return 0
      ;;
    *"@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*|*"server-sequential-thinking"*)
      printf '%s\n' "sequential-thinking-server"
      return 0
      ;;
  esac
  return 1
}

class_index() {
  local class="$1"
  local i
  for i in "${!min_classes[@]}"; do
    if [ "${min_classes[$i]}" = "$class" ]; then
      printf '%s\n' "$i"
      return 0
    fi
  done
  return 1
}

remember_min_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local age="$2"
  local pid="$3"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  if [ -z "$idx" ]; then
    min_classes+=("$class")
    min_ages+=("$age")
    min_pids+=("$pid")
    return
  fi
  if [ "$age" -lt "${min_ages[$idx]}" ]; then
    min_ages[$idx]="$age"
    min_pids[$idx]="$pid"
  fi
}

freshest_pid_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  [ -n "$idx" ] || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "${min_pids[$idx]}"
}

freshest_age_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  [ -n "$idx" ] || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "${min_ages[$idx]}"
}

pid_in_list() {
  local needle="$1"
  shift
  local item
  for item in "$@"; do
    [ "$item" = "$needle" ] && return 0
  done
  return 1
}

append_descendants() {
  local parent="$1"
  local child
  while read -r child; do
    [ -n "$child" ] || continue
    if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -eq 0 ] || ! pid_in_list "$child" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
      session_tree_pids+=("$child")
      append_descendants "$child"
    fi
  done <<EOF
$(pgrep -P "$parent" 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
}

# Build the live operator-session process tree across ALL AI CLI runtimes, not just
# Claude Code. Any MCP helper descended from a live claude / opencode / codex
# session belongs to an active operator session and must be preserved — most importantly
# an operator's `opencode run`, which previously fell outside the Claude-only tree and
# got swept after ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC (a hard-rule violation).
build_session_trees() {
  local pid
  while read -r pid; do
    [ -n "$pid" ] || continue
    if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -eq 0 ] || ! pid_in_list "$pid" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
      session_tree_pids+=("$pid")
      append_descendants "$pid"
    fi
  done <<EOF
$(pgrep -f '(^|/)(claude|opencode|codex)( |$)|Claude Code' 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
}

has_non_mcp_listener() {
  local pid="$1"
  local line
  while read -r line; do
    case "$line" in
      *LISTEN*)
        return 0
        ;;

  local freshest_age

esac
  done <<EOF
$(lsof -nP -a -p "$pid" -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF

preserve_reason() {
  local pid="$1"
  local class="$2"
  local age="$3"
  local cmd="$4"
  local freshest_pid
  local freshest_age

  # Operator-owned interactive/dispatch sessions across runtimes. These commands name a
  # session the operator is actively running; their MCP helpers must never be swept.
  # build_session_trees covers descendants via process ancestry; these patterns also
  # catch the session's own root process whatever its descendants look like.
  case "$cmd" in
    *"opencode run"*) printf '%s\n' "operator-opencode-preserve"; return 0 ;;
    *"codex exec"*) printf '%s\n' "operator-codex-preserve"; return 0 ;;
    *"ollama runner"*|*"ollama serve"*) printf '%s\n' "ollama-preserve"; return 0 ;;
  esac

  if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -gt 0 ] && pid_in_list "$pid" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
    printf '%s\n' "live-session-tree"
    return 0
  fi

  freshest_pid="$(freshest_pid_for_class "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  freshest_age="$(freshest_age_for_class "$class" 2>/dev/null || echo 999999)"
  if [ "$pid" = "$freshest_pid" ] && [ "$freshest_age" -lt "$AGE_MIN_SEC" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "freshest-young-instance"
    return 0
  fi

  if has_non_mcp_listener "$pid"; then
    printf '%s\n' "active-non-mcp-tcp-listener"
    return 0
  fi

  return 1
}

scan_processes() {
  local pid ppid etime rss stat command class age
  while read -r pid ppid etime rss stat command; do
    [ -n "${pid:-}" ] || continue
    class="$(classify_command "${command:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    [ -n "$class" ] || continue
    age="$(etime_to_seconds "$etime")"
    candidate_pids+=("$pid")
    candidate_ppids+=("$ppid")
    candidate_classes+=("$class")
    candidate_ages+=("$age")
    candidate_etimes+=("$etime")
    candidate_rsses+=("$rss")
    candidate_commands+=("${command:-}")
    remember_min_for_class "$class" "$age" "$pid"
  done <<EOF
$(ps -axo pid=,ppid=,etime=,rss=,stat=,command=)
EOF
}

select_kill_candidates() {
  local i pid class age etime rss cmd reason
  for i in "${!candidate_pids[@]}"; do
    pid="${candidate_pids[$i]}"
    class="${candidate_classes[$i]}"
    age="${candidate_ages[$i]}"
    etime="${candidate_etimes[$i]}"
    rss="${candidate_rsses[$i]}"
    cmd="${candidate_commands[$i]}"

    reason="$(preserve_reason "$pid" "$class" "$age" "$cmd" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [ -n "$reason" ]; then
      log_verbose "action=preserve reason=$reason pid=$pid class=$class rss_kb=$rss age_sec=$age etime=$etime command=$cmd"
      continue
    fi

    kill_pids+=("$pid")
    kill_classes+=("$class")
    kill_ages+=("$age")
    kill_etimes+=("$etime")
    kill_rsses+=("$rss")
    kill_commands+=("$cmd")
  done
}

terminate_candidates() {
  local i pid class age etime rss cmd
  for i in "${!kill_pids[@]}"; do
    pid="${kill_pids[$i]}"
    class="${kill_classes[$i]}"
    age="${kill_ages[$i]}"
    etime="${kill_etimes[$i]}"
    rss="${kill_rsses[$i]}"
    cmd="${kill_commands[$i]}"

    log_action "action=kill signal=TERM pid=$pid class=$class rss_kb=$rss age_sec=$age etime=$etime command=$cmd"
    if [ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]; then
      if ! kill -15 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        emit "action=kill-failed signal=TERM pid=$pid class=$class"
        EXIT_CODE=1
      fi
    fi
  done

  [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ] && return 0
  [ "${#kill_pids[@]}" -eq 0 ] && return 0

  sleep 5
  for i in "${!kill_pids[@]}"; do
    pid="${kill_pids[$i]}"
    class="${kill_classes[$i]}"
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      emit "action=kill signal=KILL pid=$pid class=$class"
      if ! kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        emit "action=kill-failed signal=KILL pid=$pid class=$class"
        EXIT_CODE=1
      fi
    fi
  done
}

path_mtime_epoch() {
  local path="$1"
  stat -f %m "$path" 2>/dev/null || stat -c %Y "$path" 2>/dev/null || printf '%s\n' 0
}

path_is_old_enough() {
  local path="$1"
  local now_epoch mtime age min_age
  now_epoch="$(date +%s)"
  mtime="$(path_mtime_epoch "$path")"
  is_positive_int "$mtime" || return 1
  age=$((now_epoch - mtime))
  min_age=$((TMP_AGE_HOURS * 3600))
  [ "$age" -ge "$min_age" ]
}

preserve_tmp_path() {
  local path="$1"
  local base="${path##*/}"
  case "$base" in
    codex-browser-use|*cache*|*Cache*) return 0 ;;
  esac
  return 1
}

remove_tmp_path() {
  local path="$1"
  [ -e "$path" ] || return 0
  preserve_tmp_path "$path" && { log_verbose "action=preserve-tmp path=$path"; return 0; }
  path_is_old_enough "$path" || { log_verbose "action=preserve-tmp reason=too-new path=$path"; return 0; }

  log_action "action=remove-tmp path=$path"
  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]; then
    if ! rm -rf "$path" 2>/dev/null; then
      emit "action=remove-tmp-failed path=$path"
      EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
  fi
}

sweep_tmp() {
  local path
  shopt -s nullglob
  for path in /tmp/codex-* /tmp/cli-* /tmp/opencode-* /tmp/deep-review-*; do
    [ -d "$path" ] || continue
    remove_tmp_path "$path"
  done
  for path in /tmp/save-context-data-* /tmp/*-prompt.md /tmp/*-cli-err.log /tmp/*-cli-out.log; do
    [ -f "$path" ] || continue
    remove_tmp_path "$path"
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
}

rotate_log_if_needed
emit "action=start dry_run=$DRY_RUN verbose=$VERBOSE age_min_sec=$AGE_MIN_SEC tmp_age_hours=$TMP_AGE_HOURS"
build_session_trees
scan_processes
select_kill_candidates
terminate_candidates
sweep_tmp
emit "action=summary candidates=${#candidate_pids[@]} kills=${#kill_pids[@]} exit_code=$EXIT_CODE"
exit "$EXIT_CODE"

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
	<!--
	  Orphan MCP sweeper LaunchAgent - TEMPLATE ONLY.

	  This file is NOT installed or loaded by default. It lives in-repo as a
	  reviewable template. To activate (operator-approved rollout only, AFTER
	  reviewing dry-run output on this machine):

	    cp .opencode/scripts/launchagents/com.michelkerkmeester.orphan-sweep.plist \
	       ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.michelkerkmeester.orphan-sweep.plist
	    launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.michelkerkmeester.orphan-sweep.plist

	  This template runs the sweeper in --dry-run mode, so loading it as-is is
	  non-destructive: it logs the kill/remove decisions it WOULD make without
	  killing any PID or deleting any /tmp file. To make it actually reap, an
	  operator must remove the <string>--dry-run</string> entry below.

	  Edit the absolute paths below if your repo root or home directory differs:
	  launchd requires absolute paths (no ~ or $HOME expansion in plists).
	-->

	<key>Label</key>
	<string>com.michelkerkmeester.orphan-sweep</string>

	<key>ProgramArguments</key>
	<array>
		<string>/bin/bash</string>
		<string>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/scripts/orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh</string>
		<string>--dry-run</string>
		<string>--verbose</string>
		<string>--log-path</string>
		<string>/Users/michelkerkmeester/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log</string>
	</array>

	<key>WorkingDirectory</key>
	<string>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public</string>

	<!-- Run every 600 seconds, matching the documented template cadence. -->
	<key>StartInterval</key>
	<integer>600</integer>

	<!-- Template posture: do not fire on load; operator activates deliberately. -->
	<key>RunAtLoad</key>
	<false/>

	<key>StandardOutPath</key>
	<string>/Users/michelkerkmeester/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.launchd.out.log</string>

	<key>StandardErrorPath</key>
	<string>/Users/michelkerkmeester/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.launchd.err.log</string>

	<!-- Low-impact background scheduling for a periodic maintenance sweep. -->
	<key>ProcessType</key>
	<string>Background</string>

	<key>LowPriorityIO</key>
	<true/>

	<key>Nice</key>
	<integer>5</integer>
</dict>
</plist>

[{"id":"F1","severity":"P2","file":".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs","line":"1449","title":"Shutdown-path comment still describes re-election as default-off","why":"The runtime behavior and unit test now make daemon re-election default-on, but this shutdown-path comment still says default off. That can mislead future reviewers/operators reading the release-vs-kill branch, although the code itself gates correctly through daemonReelectionEnabled().","evidence":".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:1449 says `Re-election release path (default off)`, while lines 205-206 return true unless SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION is exactly `0` or `off`, and line 1453 uses that helper to enter the release branch. The current unit test asserts default-on at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-daemon-reelection.vitest.ts:13-20.","recommendation":"Update the parenthetical to default-on or remove it so the comment matches the current lifecycle contract."}]
===FINDINGS_END===
